note: 1) 박스: questions.pdf 는 ~1.079pt 선; stem≠questions 인 PDF 는 0.18~0.92pt 대역(혼합 굵기). 2) problem_area: 정수(문항 번호)로 시작. 3) image_area: 이미지|image 로 시작하는 텍스트 및 해당 구간 이미지 블록. 4) image_description_area: ■ 로 시작, 정답·Answer·正确答案·Đáp án đúng 블록 아님. 5) explanation_area: 위 정답 라벨로 시작; 이후 이미지는 *_ex.png. 이미지 설명용 그림은 *_desc.png. 두 번째 595→959. 6) 동영상·웹 참조 마커 시 category=동영상 문제; video_area.file 은 questions_videos/questions_videos_<문항번호>.mp4 (문항번호 있을 때), links 는 박스 내 URL. 7) explanation_area.answer: 아라비아 숫자 또는 ①②③④; 복수 정답은 JSON 배열.

problem_area.question: Which of the following procedures is correct when applying for a mobile driver’s license?
choice: Applications can be made using any app.
Applications must be made using a smartphone under the applicant’s name with identity verification.
Applications can only be made at the traffic civil service office of a police station.
A mobile driver’s license cannot be issued at a driver’s license test center.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about driver's license issuance?
choice: A driver’s license must be issued within 30 days of passing the driver's license test.
A driver’s license cannot be issued in English.
A mobile driver’s license can be issued.
A lost driver’s license can be reissued.

problem_area.question: What is the validity period for an international driver's license issued by the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency from the date of issuance?
choice: 1 year
2 years
3 years
4 years

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is a prerequisite for driving an emergency passenger vehicle with prescribed seating capacity of no more than 15 persons?
choice: Class I driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles; 3 hours of traffic safety education
Class I driver’s license for special motor vehicles (large towing vehicles); 2 hours of traffic safety education
Class I driver’s license for special motor vehicles (recovery vehicles); 2 hours of traffic safety education
Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles; 3 hours of traffic safety education

problem_area.question: What is the period of validity of a learner’s permit according to the Road Traffic Act?
choice: 6 months from the date of issuance
1 year from the date of issuance
2 years from the date of issuance
3 years from the date of issuance

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following should NOT be indicated in a driver’s license regarding the special conditions for allowance of driver’s license?
choice: A: Manual transmission
E: Deaf sign and convex mirror
G: Specially produced and approved car
H: Right direction indicator

problem_area.question: A vehicle with total weight of 780 kg is to be towed with an 11-seater passenger vehicle. Which of the following driver’s license(s) must the driver hold?
choice: Class I driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles and for small towing vehicles
Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles and Class I driver’s license for small towing vehicles
Class I driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles and for recovery vehicles
Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles and Class I driver’s license for recovery vehicles

problem_area.question: Which TWO statements are correct regarding the types of vehicles that can be driven by specific types of driver's license?
choice: An asphalt distributor may be driven by a driver holding a Class I driver’s license for large motor vehicles.
A dump truck may be driven by a driver holding a Class I driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles.
A 250 cc two-wheeled vehicle may be driven by a person holding a Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles.
A motorcycle may be driven by a person holding a Class II driver’s license for small motor vehicles.

problem_area.question: One intends to drive a 12-seater passenger vehicle. Which of the following driver’s license types must one obtain to do so?
choice: Class I driver’s license for large towing vehicles
Class I driver’s license for recovery vehicles
Class I driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles
Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles

problem_area.question: Which of the following people can obtain a Class II driver's license for ordinary motor vehicles?
choice: Those who are blind in one eye but possess visual acuity of 0.5 in the other eye
Those who cannot differentiate red, green, and yellow colors (color blind)
Those who are 17 years old
Those who are deaf

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a correct definition (standard) of a “motorcycle” under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Displacement of up to 50 cc; maximum rated output power of up to 0.59 kW
Displacement of less than 50 cc; maximum rated output power of less than 0.59 kW
Displacement of up to 125 cc; maximum rated output power of up to 11 kW
Displacement of less than 125 cc; maximum rated output power of less than 11 kW

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following are the qualifications for taking a test for Class I driver's license for a large motor vehicle? (Excluding experience in driving a two-wheeled vehicle)
choice: A person who has been driving a vehicle for at least 6 months and who is 18 years or older
A person who has been driving a vehicle for at least 1 year and who is 18 years or older
A person who has been driving a vehicle for at least 6 months and who is 19 years or older
A person who has been driving a vehicle for at least 1 year and who is 19 years or older

problem_area.question: Which of the following is an incorrect description of postponing the driver's license aptitude test under the Road Traffic Act when traveling abroad?
choice: You must submit an application to postpone the aptitude test before leaving the country.
You cannot have an agent apply for a postponement of the aptitude test on your behalf after you leave the country.
You must provide supporting documents of your departure such as an E-ticket to apply for a postponement of the aptitude test.
If your application is approved, you must take the aptitude test within three months of your return.

problem_area.question: A person who fails the road driving test may retake the test ( ) after the date of failure. Which of the following belongs in ( )?
choice: 1 day
3 days
5 days
7 days

problem_area.question: What are TWO inappropriate explanations for the "Good Driving Mileage" system?
choice: It is a system that provides practical incentives to those who comply with and practice traffic laws.
If a driver receives a suspension order, the penalty can be deducted from the accumulated points.
Those who have not paid the traffic penalty or administrative fine can participate in the no-violation, no-accident pledge of the mileage system.
In case of causing a traffic accident or violating traffic laws during the pledge period, the person cannot take the pledge again.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT the correct definition of personal mobility devices (PMDs) in motorcycle form?
choice: The ascent angle is less than 25 degrees.
The device must weigh less than 30 kg.
The term "bicycles, etc." means bicycles and personal mobility devices.
The motor should not operate when driving at a speed of 25 km/h or more.

problem_area.question: Below are explanations of the standard for personal mobility devices (PMDs) according to the Road Traffic Act. Which one is correct?
choice: For vehicles with a motor, the motor should stop when the device reaches or exceeds a speed of 20 km/h.
It includes electric bicycles that cannot move solely by motor power (PAS: Pedal Assist System).
It is a vehicle with maximum rated output power of 11 kW or less, which weighs less than 35 kg.
The weight of the vehicle should be less than 30 kg.

problem_area.question: Which TWO actions would cause someone to be barred from acquiring a driver's license for 2 years? (If given a penalty more severe than a criminal fine)
choice: Driving without a license three times
When taking a driver’s license test on behalf of another person.
When charged for assaulting a police officer during an inspection, resulting in the revocation of one’s license.
When causing three or more traffic accidents while driving under the influence of alcohol.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about an English driver’s license? (Limited to countries that have ratified the Geneva Convention or the Vienna Convention)
choice: When driving in a country where an English driver’s license is recognized, it is possible to drive without a separate notarized translation.
The English driver's license can be used regardless of the length of stay in a country where an English driver’s license is recognized.
You should bring your Korean driver's license, an international driver's license, and your passport in a country where an English driver’s license is not recognized.
It is a license wherein the content of the driver's license is written in English on the back of the driver's license.

problem_area.question: Under the Road Traffic Act, a motorcycle is defined as a two-wheeled vehicle with maximum rated output of ( ) or less when powered by electricity. Which of the following belongs in ( )?
choice: 11 kW
9 kW
5 kW

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct definition of "line of curbstones" as stipulated in the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A line indicating the direction of the thoroughfare for vehicles and horses
A line for guiding vehicles to drive in a single line through a set part of the road
A line consisting of rocks or other material for distinguishing roadways from sidewalks
A line indicating separate lanes

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements regarding personal mobility devices under the Road Traffic Act is correct?
choice: It is possible to drive a PMD with more riders than the permitted capacity.
A person over the age of 65 who has returned his/her driver’s license can drive a PMD.
A person over the age of 13 can drive a PMD without a driver’s license.
When using a crosswalk, you must get off the personal mobility device and push or carry it while walking.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is the age standard for individuals whose driver's license renewal and aptitude test interval is 3 years?
choice: Individuals aged 65 or older
Individuals aged 70 or older
Individuals aged 75 or older
Individuals aged 80 or older

problem_area.question: Below are descriptions of the verification process for identifying individuals wishing to obtain a driver’s license according to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree. Which of the following is NOT correct?
choice: If the resident registration card has been lost, identification can be performed via the confirmation of application for the issuance of a resident registration card.
An individual who cannot be verified by either identification certificate or fingerprint information is not permitted to take the test.
Fingerprint information may be compared and verified using an electronic method without the applicant's consent.
If the applicant refuses to verify his/her own identity, the issuance of a driver’s license may be denied.

problem_area.question: Which of the following government authorities provides special education to those intending to get a license for fuel cell electric buses (FCEBs; seating capacity of 35 or more)?
choice: Korea Gas Safety Corporation
Korea Occupational Safety and Health Agency
Korea Road Traffic Authority
Korea Expressway Corporation

problem_area.question: Whom must the person likely to have his/her driver’s license suspended due to a violation of traffic laws according to the Road Traffic Act request when wishing to receive the recommended special traffic safety education? (Driving under the influence excluded)
choice: Chairperson of the Korea Road Traffic Authority
Head of the local government having jurisdiction over one's domicile
Head of a driver licensing office
Commissioner of a City/Do police agency

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, if a person who is blind in one eye intends to obtain a Class I driver's license for ordinary motor vehicles, the visual acuity of his/her other eye shall be at least ( ), he/she shall have altitudinal visual range of ( ) degrees and vertical visual range of 20 degrees, and there shall be no scotoma and hemianopia within the central visual range of 20 degrees. Which answer fits in the two ( )s?
How often is a person between 65 and 75 years of age with a Class I driver’s license (excluding persons who have lost sight in only one eye) required to take a regular aptitude test?
choice: Every 3 years
Every 5 years
Every 10 years
Every 15 years

problem_area.question: When should drivers return their driver's license to the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency? (Select TWO)
choice: When they have their driver's license revoked
When they have their driver's license suspended
When they are notified that they should take an occasional aptitude test
If they fail to take a regular aptitude test for 6 months

problem_area.question: Which of the following correctly describes the renewal period of a driver’s license under the Road Traffic Act (for drivers under 65 years of age only)?
choice: Within two months before or after one's birthday in the year that marks the tenth anniversary since passing the driver’s license test.
Within four months before or after one's birthday in the year that marks the tenth anniversary since passing the driver’s license test.
Within six months before or after one's birthday in the year that marks the tenth anniversary since passing the driver’s license test.
Within eight months before or after one's birthday in the year that marks the tenth anniversary since passing the driver’s license test.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following cannot be issued an English driver's license?
choice: A person who has passed the driver’s license test and who applies for a driver’s license
A person who has passed the driver’s license aptitude test and who applies for a driver’s license
A person who applies for exchanging a foreign driver’s license with a domestic driver’s license
A person who applies with a student license

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is not allowed to be driven with a Class II driver’s license for ordinary motor vehicles?
choice: A recovery vehicle
A passenger vehicle with seating capacity of less than 10
A passenger car
A truck with loading weight of 2.5 tons

problem_area.question: A person whose driver’s license test result becomes invalidated due to cheating shall be prohibited from taking the relevant examination for ( ) from the date of imposition. Which answer fits in ( )?
choice: 2 years
3 years
4 years
5 years

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is NOT a valid reason for postponing license renewal or the regular aptitude test?
choice: If the person is staying overseas
If the person is unable to move due to an illness
If the person is serving as a noncommissioned officer or higher in the Army, Navy, or Air Force pursuant to the Military Personnel Management Act
If the person falls victim to a disaster or a calamity

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, a person who has received an extension of the period for renewal of a driver's license must renew and obtain a driver's license within ( ) of the date such cause or event is terminated. Which of the following belongs in ( )?
choice: 1 month
3 months
6 months
12 months

problem_area.question: According to the High-pressure Gas Safety Control Act, which of the following are required to take special education?
choice: Drivers of fuel cell passenger vehicles
Drivers of large FCEBs (seating capacity of 36 or more)
Drivers of fuel cell trucks
Drivers of special-purpose FCEVs

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the obligations of a driver who has obtained a conditional driver’s license upon installing an ignition interlock device under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A person who has obtained a conditional driver’s license upon installing an ignition interlock device and who drives without registering it with the commissioner of the city or police agency of his/her municipality will have his/her license suspended.
A person who has driven a vehicle without an ignition interlock device or with such device that fails to meet the installation standards shall be ordered to take corrective measures within one month.

A person who drives a motor vehicle with an ignition interlock device, etc. knowing that the device is less effective as it was dismantled, tampered with, or otherwise for maintenance will have his/her license suspended.

After the period of license disqualification due to drunk driving, a person may only drive a vehicle with an ignition interlock device from the time of obtaining a conditional driver’s license upon installing an ignition interlock device.

problem_area.question: What is the standard for penalties imposed on a driver who knowingly uses a fake petroleum product as vehicle fuel?
choice: An administrative fine between KRW 50,000 and 100,000
An administrative fine between KRW 500,000 and 1 million
An administrative fine between KRW 2 million and 20 million
No penalties

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding charging stations of electric vehicles (EVs)?
choice: Common-use chargers refer to chargers installed in rest areas, large marts, and public offices.
For the charging method of EVs, there are slow-charging methods using alternating current and fast-charging methods using direct current.
Only pre-registered vehicles can use common-use chargers.
If one possesses land or lot, one can install a personal charging facility.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a problem that can occur in cars when refueling with fake petroleum products?
choice: Increased engine noise due to corrosion and damage of the fuel supply device
Reduced power and fuel efficiency due to damage to the injector that injects fuel
Reduced power due to reduction of engine friction caused by increased lubricity
Power-downs due to damage to the high-pressure fuel pump supplying fuel

problem_area.question: Which TWO items should you check before getting in a vehicle?
choice: Condition of tire wear
Whether there are obstacles in front of or behind your car
Whether the dashboard readings are working properly
Whether the brake pedal works normally

problem_area.question: What is the color generally displayed on the inspection screen when the life of a maintenance-free (MF) battery expires?
choice: Yellow
White
Black
Green

problem_area.question: Which of the following CANNOT be considered a fake petroleum product for use as vehicle fuel?
choice: Gasoline mixed with methanol
A product that contains less than 5% high-grade gasoline mixed with normal gasoline
Diesel oil mixed with kerosene
A product with less than 5% water mixed with diesel

problem_area.question: Which of the following is not the correct method of checking for hydrogen gas leaks?
choice: Measurement using a flammable gas detector
Confirmation through soapy water
Confirmation by smelling gas
Confirmation using a hydrogen detector

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the safety rules for hydrogen vehicles?
choice: Switch off the vehicle power before charging.
Smoking should be done at a distance from the vehicle.
When hydrogen gas leaks, request the safety supervisor from the charging station for a safety check.
After a traffic accident involving the collision of a hydrogen vehicle, drive the vehicle after undergoing a gas safety inspection.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is not necessary when checking for leaks in hydrogen vehicles?
choice: Connection between valve and container
Regulator
Gas hose and pipe connection
Boost inverter of fuel cell

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT an appropriate method of charging an EV?
choice: When charging, use a charger and an adapter that are compatible with the specifications.
During charging, do not disconnect the charging connector arbitrarily. End charging with the charging completion button.
Be careful not to use the charger with wet hands; prevent water from entering the charging device.
When using a portable charger to charge, you can use a household power strip or an extension cord.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct pairing of light and color?
choice: Reverse light – Amber
License plate light – Blue
Taillight – White
Brake light – Red

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the characteristics of the fuel used in an LPG vehicle?
choice: It burns in a gaseous state, allowing the engine to operate smoothly.
LPG for motor vehicles has a distinct odor.
It is generally lighter than air and less explosive.
It has a lower energy density compared to gasoline or diesel, resulting in lower output and lower fuel efficiency for vehicles equipped with an engine of the same capacity.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a cause of reducing the braking force of a vehicle?
choice: Master cylinder failure
Defective wheel cylinder
Deformation of release fork
Occurrence of vapor lock

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the method of driving a vehicle equipped with a driving assistance device?
choice: When using a driving assistance device, check frequently whether the device is operating while driving.
In case of a driving intervention warning, wait until the driving assistance device is released before intervening.
When fully or partially releasing the driving assistance device, check whether it works.
Even if the driving assistance system is in operation, be ready to intervene at short notice.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about a driver-assistance system?
choice: Lane Following Assist (LFA) is a system designed to assist the driver in driving to stay within the lane on the road.
Adaptive Smart Cruise Control (ASCC) is a system that automatically adjusts the vehicle speed to maintain a safe distance from vehicles ahead.
Active Blind Spot Detection (ABSD) is a system that assists drivers in changing lanes by warning them of vehicles in their blind spots.
Autonomous Emergency Braking (AEB) is a system that prevents the wheels from locking up and reduces the braking distance.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding provisions on autonomous driving systems?
choice: The Road Traffic Act stipulates that “driving” does not include the use of an autonomous driving system in accordance with the original use of motor vehicles and horses.
The prohibition of using a mobile phone does not apply to drivers driving a vehicle operated under an autonomous driving system.
The traffic penalty for a driver who fails to respond promptly to the take-over request from the autonomous driving system is a fee of KRW 40,000.

An “autonomous driving system” encompasses a variety of automated apparatus, software, and peripheral devices that allow a vehicle to move by recognizing and judging the surroundings and road information without the manipulation of the driver or a passenger.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a crucial component of an FCEV?
choice: A fuel cell
A traction motor
An engine
A battery

problem_area.question: What is the rotational force (instantaneous force) generated from the crankshaft of an internal combustion engine of a vehicle?
choice: Torque
Fuel economy
Displacement
Horsepower

problem_area.question: According to the Act on the Promotion of and Support for Commercialization of Autonomous Vehicles, which of the following is NOT true about autonomous vehicles?
choice: The types of autonomous vehicles can be divided into fully autonomous vehicles and semi-autonomous vehicles.
Fully autonomous vehicles refer to vehicles that can operate solely on autonomous driving systems without the need for a driver or any intervention from the driver or passengers.

Semi-autonomous vehicles refer to vehicles that cannot operate solely on autonomous driving systems or which require continuous attention from the driver or passengers, necessitating their intervention.
Autonomous vehicles are limited to passenger vehicles, and this law does not apply to buses or trucks.

problem_area.question: Which two statements are NOT correct regarding the management of EVs?
choice: The valid period for the vehicle inspection of non-commercial passenger vehicles is 6 years.
When driving long distances, check and charge the battery in advance.
Refrain from accelerating or suddenly stopping immediately after charging.
It is more efficient to use an air heater than a heated seat or a heated handle.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is subject to regulations regarding the permeability of visible rays through the window glass of motor vehicles (except for children’s school buses)?
choice: Window glass on the side of the back seat
Front windshield, window glass on the right and left sides of the driver's seat
Front windshield, window glass on the right and left sides of the driver's seat, and rear windshield
All windows

problem_area.question: According to the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree, a passenger car is a motor vehicle suitably manufactured to transport up to how many people?
choice: 10 people or fewer
12 people or fewer
15 people or fewer
18 people or fewer

problem_area.question: Under the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree, what is the validity period for the initial inspection conducted on a new non-commercial passenger car?
choice: 1 year
2 years
4 years
5 years

problem_area.question: Which TWO are considered types of vehicles according to the Motor Vehicle Management Act?
choice: Construction machinery
Truck
Tractor
Special motor vehicle

problem_area.question: What is the color scheme of the license plates attached to EVs and hydrogen fuel cell vehicles used for non-commercial or rental purposes (apart from hybrid vehicles)?
choice: Black letters on yellow background
Black letters on blue background
White letters on royal blue background
Black letters on purple background

problem_area.question: Which of the following vehicles CANNOT use the Hi-pass lanes?
choice: A dump truck with loading weight of 16 tons
A double-decker bus that operates between Seoul and Suwon
A fire vehicle with vehicle width of 3.7m in case of a single lane
A large recovery vehicle weighing 10 tons

problem_area.question: Under the Motor Vehicle Management Act, what is the period of validity for the inspection of a small non-commercial van (registered in or after 2001) that is over 4 years old?
choice: 6 months
1 year
2 years
4 years

problem_area.question: Under the Motor Vehicle Management Act, what is the period of validity for the inspection of a small non-commercial truck that is no more than 4 years old?
choice: 6 months
1 year
2 years
4 years

problem_area.question: Under the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree, what is the standard validity period of a temporary driving permit issued when purchasing a new car?
choice: Within 10 days
Within 15 days
Within 20 days
Within 30 days

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT accepted as a reason for change of registration of a vehicle in accordance with the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree?
choice: The main use address of the car has been changed.
The vehicle's identification number has been changed.
Ownership has been changed.
The name of the corporation has been changed.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a desirable mindset for drivers of autonomous vehicles?
choice: Since it is an autonomous vehicle, the drivers can drive even under the influence of alcohol.
The driver must not drive an autonomous vehicle in an exhausted state.
Even drivers of autonomous vehicles can take psychoactive medication and drive.
The driver may use a mobile phone while driving an autonomous vehicle.

problem_area.question: According to the Trucking Transport Business Act, the operator of a trucking transport business has to keep operation records in the tachograph for ( ). Which answer fits in ( )?
choice: 3 months
6 months
1 year
2 years

problem_area.question: Under the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree, a person who wishes to register the transfer of a vehicle must finish the registration within ( ) of the date of purchase. What belongs in ( )?
choice: 15 days
20 days
30 days
40 days

problem_area.question: According to the Motor Vehicle Management Act, the period for regular vehicle inspection is from ( ) days before to ( ) days after the expiration date of the inspection validity. Which of the following fills in the blanks correctly?

Under the Guarantee of Compulsory Motor Vehicle Liability Security Act, what is the punishment criterion for a car owner who fails to purchase mandatory insurance? (When the car has not been operated)
choice: An administrative fine of up to KRW 3 million
An administrative fine of up to KRW 5 million
Up to 1 year of imprisonment or a criminal fine of up to KRW 10 million
Up to 2 years of imprisonment or a criminalfine of up to KRW 20 million

problem_area.question: According to the Motor Vehicle Management Act, what is the registration that needs to be done when the ownership of a car is changed through inheritance or other means?
choice: New registration
Transfer of ownership registration
Modification registration
Cancellation registration

problem_area.question: According to the Motor Vehicle Management Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is NOT a type of vehicle inspection that a vehicle owner should do?
choice: Repair inspection
Special inspection
Inspection of tuning
Provisional inspection

problem_area.question: Which of the following processes the registration of transfer for a vehicle sale?
choice: Korea Road Traffic Authority
Si, Gun, or Gu district office
Korea Transportation Safety Authority
City/Do (Province) police agency

problem_area.question: Which TWO are NOT types of vehicle registration?
choice: Registration of revision
Registration of rights
Registration of patent rights
Registration of cancellation

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the appropriate permeability of visible rays through the front windshield of motor vehicles (other than children’s school buses)?
What is the proper sequence of applying brakes while driving?
choice: Identify hazard → make judgment → order action → apply brakes
Identify hazard → order action → make judgment → apply brakes
Make judgment → identify hazard → order action → apply brakes
Order action → identify hazard → make judgment → apply brakes

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a required condition for an airbag?
choice: Tensile strength and heat resistance at a high temperature
Tensile strength and heat resistance at a low temperature
Rupture strength, wear resistance, flexibility
Maximizing the impact energy in contact with a driver

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the precautions to take when getting in and out of a car?
choice: When getting in or out, check if there is a car coming from behind.
Get out of the car immediately after completely opening the door.
When a person exits a vehicle from the back seat, the driver should look straight forward.
When leaving the driver's seat temporarily, you need not turn off the car's engine.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following correctly connects the situation and the proper action that drivers must take?
choice: School zone - Honk to inform children of the vehicle passing by.
Railroad crossing - Increase speed to pass quickly when a crossing barrier is about to go down.
Intersection without signals - When turning right, drive slowly along the right side of the road and turn right.
Night driving - Adjust the headlights to high beam to caution drivers coming from the opposite direction.

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements about overtaking is correct under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Inside tunnels, overtaking is allowed during daytime but banned during nighttime.
Drivers can overtake the car ahead either on its left or on its right after turning on the headlights and honking.
Overtaking is not allowed on bridges or at intersections.
A driver may stop, proceed slowly, or overtake a vehicle as directed by a police officer.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a desirable driver attitude?
choice: Because the traffic situation is not changing, there is no need to make plans ahead.
Equip the vehicle with a fire extinguisher to take action immediately in case of fire.
Remove lighters and other flammable items from the vehicle.
Drive courteously, especially for novice drivers.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most appropriate driving behavior for a driver?
choice: Drowsy driving entails the risk of a traffic accident, so it is important to stop the car on a roadside to rest when you are drowsy.
Novice drivers should continuously drive in the overtaking lane on an expressway.
Install and operate a device that interferes with the functions of traffic regulation equipment.
Upon identifying a risk to traffic safety, inform others with emergency lights.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a desirable driver attitude?
choice: Always drive while obeying traffic rules, such as driving under the speed limit.
Drive on the shoulder in the event of traffic congestion.
Compliance with traffic laws and regulations is a mutual commitment among drivers.
Put pedestrian safety first before increasing the vehicle speed.

problem_area.question: In case of an accident, which of the following is NOT considered the responsibility of the driver?
choice: Criminal liability
Administrative liability
Civil liability
Notification liability

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct response by a driver to a traffic accident while driving on an expressway?
choice: Take first-aid measures depending on the severity of a passenger's injury.
Turn on the hazard lights to alert trailing drivers of the danger.
Control the traffic at the rear part of the vehicle until a police officer arrives.
Move to a safer place to prevent secondary accidents.

problem_area.question: Which of the following actions is the correct behavior of a driver driving a passenger vehicle with an infant?
choice: An adult in the passenger seat sits with an infant and wears the same seat belt.
A seat belt is enough for an infant sitting in the back seat; an infant safety harness is not necessary.
To appease a crying infant while driving, it is OK to let the infant sit together with the driver in the driver’s seat and wear the same seatbelt.
When riding with an infant, always use an infant safety harness with a seat belt fastened, regardless of the road taken by the driver.

problem_area.question: Which TWO are the responsibilities of a driver?
choice: Stop the vehicle if there is risk of a traffic accident involving children.
You must slow down when riding over a puddle so as not to splash others.
Apply dark tinting since the brightness of vehicle windows is not regulated.
Drive at a reduced speed when pedestrians are crossing a crosswalk.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are desirable behaviors when driving on a freeway? (Select TWO)
choice: If you are drowsy or exhausted from driving, pull the vehicle over on the shoulder to take a rest or a nap.
You can watch entertaining videos while driving.
You should take a rest and ventilate the vehicle regularly to stay awake.
You should constantly check for hazards along the route before departure and even while taking a rest.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT desirable regarding a driver's necessary preparation for safe driving?
choice: Control your body condition so that your attention is not diverted.
Wear clothes that are comfortable and suitable for driving.
Prepare emergency signaling devices such as flare.
In order to save fuel, preheat the engine by turning it on 10 minutes before departure.

problem_area.question: Which TWO are correct regarding concentration while driving?
choice: Continuously chatting with a passenger while driving improves the driver's concentration.
It is better to remove stickers that can block the driver’s view.
Concentration while driving has nothing to do with safe driving.
TVs or DMBs should be installed where they can be seen only by passengers in the back seat.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, if an infant is on board a vehicle (except for two-wheeled vehicles), using an infant safety harness is required. Which of the following is the age criteria for an infant?
choice: 8 years or below
Below 8 years
Below 6 years
6 years or below

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about regulations and proper operation of PMDs?
choice: The driver must turn on headlights and daytime running lights when driving at night.
Among the PMDs, not more than one person can drive an electric kick scooter.
A personal mobility device includes self-balancing scooters, electric kick scooters, electric bicycles, electric unicycles, and mobility scooters.
An electric bicycle powered by a motor can carry two persons.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is true regarding wearing a seat belt before driving a vehicle (excluding two-wheeled vehicles)?
choice: If the passenger is a child under 13 years old and is not wearing a seat belt, the driver will be slapped with an administrative fine of KRW 100,000.
If the passenger is 13 years old or above and is not wearing a seat belt, the driver will be slapped with an administrative fine of KRW 30,000.
Only the driver and front passenger are obligated to wear seat belts when driving on general roads.
All passengers are obligated to wear seat belts, but a child under 3 years old can be placed on the lap of the guardian.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a good driving attitude for accident prevention?
choice: Using indicators to make sure that the others know the driver’s path of travel
Frequently making sudden maneuvers and braking
Driving to get through traffic as advantageously as possible for oneself
Retribution for aggressive driving by other drivers

problem_area.question: Which of the following actions is NOT desirable for a driver?
choice: Drive according to the flow of traffic within the speed limit.
If you are a novice driver, use the shoulder on the expressway and do not interfere with the traffic flow.
Do not fight without stopping your car on the road first.
A student license holder should drive with a companion according to the law.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following best describes "yielding"?
choice: When driving continuously at slow speed, the driver must yield the lane by moving to the far left side of the road.
When an emergency vehicle is approaching from behind, the driver must proceed quickly.
At an intersection without a traffic light, the driver must yield the right of way to vehicles approaching from the left.
Vehicles driving on a road with a yield sign must yield the lane to vehicles on other roads.

problem_area.question: According to the Act on the Promotion of Transportation Convenience of Mobility Disadvantaged Persons, who among the following are NOT considered “mobility disadvantaged persons”?
choice: The elderly
Pregnant women
Persons accompanied by infants
Persons with pets

problem_area.question: Under the Act on the Promotion of Transportation Convenience of Mobility Disadvantaged Persons, which of the following is not considered a “pedestrian safety facility” for mobility disadvantaged persons?
choice: Speed reduction facility
Bicycle lane
Transportation information facilities such as public traffic information system
Traffic signal for pedestrians' right of way

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following cases requires driving at a slow speed?
choice: When the traffic signal at the intersection is flashing in red
Upon passing an intersection where traffic is not controlled and is frequent
Upon passing an intersection where traffic is not controlled
Upon changing lanes near an intersection

problem_area.question: What is the best way of proceeding when turning left at a congested intersection?
choice: If possible, enter the intersection while following the car in front.
You may enter the intersection if the traffic light is green.
If there is space available, you may enter the intersection even if the traffic light is red.
Do not enter the intersection even when there is a green arrow.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of entering a travel lane from an acceleration lane of an expressway?
choice: Stop temporarily to check the traffic flow, and then quickly enter the expressway.
Stop temporarily before entering the expressway and abruptly enter when there are vehicles passing on the road.
Enter the expressway even if there is not much space to enter, in consideration of the vehicle behind.
Use the acceleration lane, maintaining a certain speed, and enter the expressway when enough space is secured.

problem_area.question: Vehicle A is moving slowly on the right side of the expressway's main lane. In this case, what is the best method of entering the main lane?
choice: Slowly increase the speed and enter, but make sure you do so after Vehicle A passes.
Accelerate and enter using an empty roadside.
Stop at the end of the acceleration lane and enter after Vehicle A passes.
Continue driving on the acceleration lane at the same speed as Vehicle A.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most appropriate driving behavior when a driver notices a child crossing the road without a guardian?
choice: Repeatedly honk so that the child can cross quickly.
Ensure the safety of the child crossing the road by moving slowly.
Temporarily stop and ensure the safety of the child crossing the road.
Ensure the safety of the child crossing the road by passing quickly.

problem_area.question: What is the safest driving method when entering an intersection with no traffic lights and where both the left and right sides are not visible?
choice: Drive slowly depending on the surrounding situation and pass through the intersection after checking that it is safe.
Enter while honking and flashing headlights, and drive slowly while passing through.
Make a temporary stop to make sure that it is safe, and then pass through the intersection according to right-of-way standards.
Since a vehicle entering the intersection first has priority, quickly pass through the intersection while checking the surroundings.

problem_area.question: Which of the following would cause the biggest safety hazard when turning left at an intersection?
choice: A pedestrian crossing a crosswalk on the right side of the intersection
A motorcycle approaching at high speed from the rear in the right roadway
A passenger car turning right from the left side of the intersection
A bicycle turning right from the opposite side

problem_area.question: Under the Road Traffic Act, which of the following describes the safest attitude for a person driving a personal mobility device?
choice: When passing on a sidewalk, drive away from pedestrians.
When driving after drinking alcohol, special attention is required.
If there are a crosswalk and a bicycle crossing, use the bicycle crossing.
When driving the device on a crosswalk, avoid pedestrians on the crosswalk.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the traffic safety policy referred to as Safety Speed 5030?
choice: Within 50 km per hour on the automobile road and 30 km per hour on the back road in a residential area in a city
Within 50 km per hour on the main road in a city and 30 km per hour on the back road in a residential area in a city
Within 50 km per hour on the automobile road and 30 km per hour in a school zone
Within 50 km per hour on the main road in a city and 30 km per hour on the bicycle track

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, what are TWO situations or locations where driving at slow speed is required?
choice: An intersection without any traffic signals
A road with children crossing without a guardian
A road where you see a blind person crossing using a white cane
The start of a winding road

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the proper ways of driving around a roundabout? (Select TWO)
choice: Vehicles already in the roundabout have priority.
Enter a roundabout as quickly as possible.
Turn on the hazard lights when entering a roundabout.
The driving direction in a roundabout is counterclockwise.

problem_area.question: Which TWO statements are correct regarding driving on an expressway?
choice: All passengers must wear seat belts on an expressway.
A vehicle driving on an expressway must yield to merging vehicles.
When driving on an expressway, you are not obliged to yield even if an emergency vehicle approaches.
A vehicle breakdown sign (including a safety tripod sign) must be carried in the vehicle.

problem_area.question: Which TWO descriptions are correct?
choice: The road surface marking for the "yield" instruction is indicated as a white ＂△＂ sign.
Cars driving in a lane with the yield mark must yield to cars driving in other lanes.
On a road, you must give a lane change signal 30 m before changing lanes.
In order to maintain smoother traffic flow, you must narrow the distance with the car in front of you by increasing your speed.

problem_area.question: Which TWO statements are correct regarding yielding at an intersection with no traffic control?
choice: When turning left, you must yield to vehicles going straight or turning right at the intersection.
If you are about to enter the intersection, you are not required to yield to any vehicles that are already inside the intersection and which are turning left.
If you are about to enter the intersection, you must not yield to any vehicles that are about to enter the intersection from a narrower road.
When multiple vehicles assigned the same priority are about to enter the intersection at the same time, the vehicles on the right have the right of way.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which TWO statements are NOT correct regarding personal mobility devices (PMDs)?
choice: The electric motor must not operate when traveling at speeds of over 25 km/h, and the device must weigh less than 30 kg.
Anyone aged 13 or older may operate one without a license.
A motor-driven bicycle powered solely by a motor is classified as a personal mobility device under the law.
Personal mobility devices may freely use both bicycle lanes and sidewalks.

problem_area.question: Which of the following drivers is most likely to cause an accident?
choice: A driver who is focused only on driving
A driver with a habit of sudden starts, sudden braking, and sudden lane changes
A driver who keeps a safe distance from bicycles and two-wheeled vehicles
A driver who is a patient

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT desirable driving behavior?
choice: Keep in mind that a police officer's hand signals overrule traffic lights.
Stay updated with the ever-changing traffic laws and regulations.
Upon identifying an emergency vehicle, immediately stop the vehicle regardless of your location, and make way.
Watch out for potholes when driving on a road where heavy rainfall is common or during the rainy season.

problem_area.question: You are about to enter an uncontrolled intersection to drive straight through. When a vehicle turning left from the opposite lane has already entered the intersection, what is the correct way of driving?
choice: Yield to the other vehicle already in the intersection.
Straight traffic takes the right of way over turning traffic. Drive through.
Ignore the vehicle in the intersection; check both sides and quickly drive through.
If your lane is wider than that of another vehicle in the intersection, you have the right of way.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about the passenger capacity of personal mobility devices?
choice: An electric kick scooter can carry one person.
A self-balancing scooter can carry one person.
An electric bicycle powered by a motor can carry one person.
The traffic fine for violating the passenger capacity of a personal mobility device is KRW 40,000.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a good attitude for a driver?
choice: Respect for communication and safety
Giving precedence to cars over people
Prioritizing his/her own car over others' vehicles
Accidents are a product of bad luck, not unlawful driving

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is incorrect regarding drivers who have been issued a conditional driver’s license requiring the installation of an alcohol ignition interlock device?
choice: When intending to drive a vehicle, the driver must install an alcohol ignition interlock device and register it with the commissioner of the city or provincial police.
Driving a vehicle that is not equipped with an alcohol ignition interlock device is prohibited.
Driving a vehicle with an alcohol ignition interlock device that does not meet the installation standards is permitted.
The driver must submit the driving records of the vehicle with the alcohol ignition interlock device to the commissioner of the city or provincial police at least twice a year.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following correctly describes the penalty imposed on a driver who cannot operate a vehicle properly due to fatigue (including drowsy driving)?
choice: No penalty
Criminal fine of up to KRW 100,000 or detention
Criminal fine of up to KRW 200,000 or detention
Criminal fine of up to KRW 300,000 or detention

problem_area.question: Fatigue affects a driver's driving performance. Which of the following correctly describes the influence of fatigue on a driver?
choice: The response time of a driver to stimuli in his/her surroundings quickens.
The vision of the driver deteriorates and his/her view widens.
A driver's ability to recognize objects and drive safely deteriorates.
A driver's driving performance becomes more meticulous and planned.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a correct explanation of the punishment criteria for drunk driving?
choice: In case of the first violation, if the blood alcohol concentration is 0.2% or more, imprisonment for 2 to 5 years or criminal fine of KRW 10 million - 20 million
Refusing a field sobriety test once may result in imprisonment with labor for 1 year or more but not more than 5 years, or criminal fine of KRW 5 million or more but not more than KRW 20 million
In case of a second violation involving a blood alcohol concentration of 0.05%, imprisonment for up to 1 year or criminal fine of up to KRW 5 million

Upon the first violation at a blood alcohol level of at least 0.08% but less than 0.2%: imprisonment with labor for at least 1 year but not more than 2 years or criminal fine of at least KRW 5 million but not more than KRW 10 million

problem_area.question: If you drive when you are tired, you will not be able to sense correctly the speed at which you are driving. Which of the following is correct?
choice: On a narrow road, you will feel as if you are going slower than you actually are.
On roads with wide-open views, you will feel as if you are going faster than you actually are.
You could underestimate the speed of a car coming toward you from far away and end up in an accident.
There is no possibility of you mistaking a car stopped ahead on an expressway to be moving.

problem_area.question: Which TWO may affect free running distance when driving a car?
choice: Driving on a rainy day
Driving under the influence of alcohol
Driving when the vehicle has insufficient brake fluid
Driving when tired

problem_area.question: Which TWO statements are correct regarding penalties imposed on drunk drivers?
choice: Fully inebriated drivers with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.08% or higher will have their driver's license revoked and receive punishment in accordance with the law.

Those who refuse to take the alcohol breathalyzer test or who have caused fatalities or injuries while driving with a blood alcohol concentration of 0.03% or higher will have their driver's license revoked and receive punishment in accordance with the law.

Those who are caught driving with a blood alcohol concentration of at least 0.03 percent but less than 0.08 percent will have their driver's license suspended for 120 days and receive punishment in accordance with the law.

In a case wherein the driver causes an accident for the first time resulting in property damage while driving with a blood alcohol concentration of at least 0.03 percent but less than 0.08 percent, his/her driver’s license is revoked.

problem_area.question: Which TWO statements are correct regarding drunk driving?
choice: If a person disagrees with the result of a breathalyzer test, he/she may undergo the test again.
The Road Traffic Act does not contain any provision for punishing acts that interfere with alcohol testing.
A person who interferes with an alcohol test after riding a bicycle while drunk may be subject to punishment.

A driver of a motor vehicle who refuses to comply with a police officer’s request to take an alcohol test, despite being reasonably suspected of being intoxicated, will have his/her driver’s license revoked.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are true about drowsy and/or fatigued driving? (Select TWO)
choice: If drowsy while driving, you must consume more caffeine and change lanes more often to avoid monotonous driving.
Your alertness improves on roads with fewer variations and potential hazards, thereby reducing the risk of drowsy driving.
Avoid driving if you have taken cold medicines since they may cause drowsiness.
Alcohol deactivates certain cerebral functions and increases the chance of drowsiness.

problem_area.question: What penalty is a driver subjected to if caught by the police while driving in a physical state of being unable to drive normally (due to illness or fatigue)?
choice: Administrative fine
Suspension of the driver's license
Detention or criminal fine
No penalty

problem_area.question: Under the Act on Special Cases Concerning the Settlement of Traffic Accidents, what is the correct legal responsibility of a driver who causes a traffic accident resulting in injury or death while driving a vehicle in a condition of impaired normal driving due to drug or other substance abuse?
choice: The driver will be criminally charged despite being covered by liability car insurance and even after having settled with the accident victim(s).
The driver will be exempted from criminal charges if he/she is covered by car insurance.
The driver will be exempted from criminal charges if he/she is covered by comprehensive insurance.
The driver will be exempted from criminal charges if he/she is covered by comprehensive insurance and he/she has settled with the accident victim(s).

problem_area.question: Driver A, with blood alcohol concentration of over 0.03%, is rear-ended by Driver B while waiting at a red light. What happens next?
choice: Since drunk driving is a serious offense, Driver A should be penalized.
Although Driver B needs to be penalized for the accident, Driver A is also penalized separately for drunk driving.
Driver A is the victim in this accident and will not be subject to administrative penalties.
Driver B receives no demerit points regardless of the cause or result of the accident.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, a driver with blood alcohol concentration of ( ) or more is prohibited from driving. Select one of the following to fill in the brackets:
choice: 0.01% or higher
0.02% or higher
0.03% or higher
0.08% or higher

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO correct descriptions with regard to fatigued driving and driving under the influence of drugs?
choice: Fatigued driving is not likely to lead to falling asleep at the wheel.
Fatigued driving is dangerous because it leads to poor focus, judgment, and reflexes.
Driving under the influence of drugs and causing an accident resulting in injury are subject to punishment.
If your driver's license is revoked because of a hit-and-run event while driving under the influence of drugs, it is possible to get the license back after three years.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT an appropriate method of preventing retaliatory driving?
choice: Turn on the emergency flashers in case of emergency braking.
Turn off the head-beam headlights when a car approaches from the opposite direction.
Turn on the high-beam headlights when driving at high speed.
Wait for three seconds if the vehicle in front does not start moving right away.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT an appropriate way of reporting retaliatory driving?
choice: Calling 120
Calling 112
Reporting to the smartphone app Safety e-Report
Reporting the incident to the Cyber Police Agency

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, drivers of ( ) are prohibited from driving their motor vehicles and the like in a row or side by side without good cause and in a manner that endangers other persons and causes danger to traffic. Anyone who violates this provision will be punished by ( ). Choose the correct pair for the parentheses.
choice: self-balancing scooters - imprisonment with labor for not more than 1 year or criminal fine of not more than KRW 5 million
two-wheeled vehicles - imprisonment with labor for not more than 6 months or criminal fine of not more than KRW 3 million
special-purpose vehicles -imprisonment with labor for not more than 2 years or criminal fine of not more than KRW 5 million
motorcycles - imprisonment with labor for not more than 6 months or criminal fine of not more than KRW 3 million

problem_area.question: You may be subject to a penalty according to the Criminal Act if you overtake a vehicle over the centerline or perform any retaliatory driving such as sudden braking. In this case, what is the correct punishment that will be imposed on you?
choice: You will be sentenced to imprisonment for up to 7 years or criminal fine of not more than KRW 10 million.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for up to 10 years or criminal fine of not more than KRW 20 million.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term of more than 1 year.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term of more than 18 months.

problem_area.question: If a passenger vehicle driver responds to a lane-change dispute by suddenly braking in front of the pursuing vehicle, leading to a subsequent collision with an unaware cargo truck and resulting in injury to the cargo truck driver, legal repercussions may arise under the provisions of the Criminal Act for engaging in retaliatory driving. What is the appropriate punishment standard for this? (The cargo truck driver is not seriously injured)
choice: You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term in the range of 1 year - 10 years.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term in the range of 1 year - 20 years.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term in the range of 2 years - 10 years.
You will be sentenced to imprisonment for a limited term in the range of 2 years - 20 years.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is not subject to legal action for reckless driving under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Violating the maximum speed limit
Violating a prohibition of crossing the road, making a U-turn, or moving backward
Cutting in a lane
Honking continuously

problem_area.question: A driver of a motor vehicle (excluding personal mobility devices) threatening another driver by committing the acts below in a repeated manner will be punished for reckless driving. Which of the following is NOT an example of reckless driving?
choice: Violation of traffic signals or instructions
Violation of prohibitions on crossing, making a U-turn, and driving backward
Creating noise without reasonable cause
Violating a designated lane of an expressway

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is the correct punishment for reckless driving?
choice: Notice for payment of traffic penalty of KRW 60,000
Administrative fine of KRW 30,000
Imprisonment for up to 6 months or criminal fine of not more than KRW 2 million
Imprisonment for up to 1 year or criminal fine of not more than KRW 5 million

problem_area.question: What is the most incorrect action by the driver when a vehicle's cargo (including the driver's vehicle) falls into the lane while driving on an expressway?
choice: Stop the vehicle in a safe place while checking for vehicles behind.
Report the case promptly to the relevant authorities such as expressway management agency.
Stop the vehicle in a safe place and check the cargo loading status.
Conduct retaliatory driving against the driver who dropped the cargo.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following cannot be construed as reckless driving for a motorcycle (excluding personal mobility devices)?
choice: The driver repeatedly ignored traffic signals three times.
The driver committed successive violations of exceeding the speed limit and disobeying instructions.
The driver committed successive violations of ignoring traffic signals and crossing the centerline.
The driver committed successive violations of crossing the centerline as well as the duty to protect pedestrians.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding reckless driving and retaliatory driving?
choice: A motorcycle driver who generates noise without just cause to threaten many unspecified drivers can be penalized for retaliatory driving.
The driver of a passenger car who drives over the centerline and exceeds the speed limit repeatedly to threaten many unspecified drivers can be penalized for reckless driving.
The driver of a large truck who intentionally overtakes a car and brakes suddenly can be penalized for reckless driving.
A bus driver who repeatedly violates the legal way of overtaking a car, thereby increasing the risk of traffic accident, can be penalized for retaliatory driving.

problem_area.question: Causing harm to the life and safety of others by repeatedly driving over the centerline or causing risk in the traffic flow are ( ) according to the Road Traffic Act. Which of the following fits in ( )?
choice: joint risky behavior
reckless driving
violent driving
retaliatory driving

problem_area.question: A driver of a motor vehicle (excluding personal mobility devices) threatening another driver by committing the acts below in a repeated manner on a general road will be punished for reckless driving. Which of the following is NOT an example of reckless driving?
choice: Violating a designated lane on a general road
Driving over the centerline and violating the prohibition on sudden braking
Not complying with the safe distance, violating lane-changing prohibitions
Improperly overtaking a car on a general road

problem_area.question: A driver of a motor vehicle (excluding personal mobility devices) threatening another person by repeatedly committing a series of two or more of the acts below will be punished for reckless driving. Which of the following is NOT an example of reckless driving?
choice: Not keeping the eyes forward; operating a video display device while driving
Exceeding the speed limit while overtaking a car on his/her right side
Not securing sufficient safe distance and repeating sudden braking
Exceeding the speed limit to obstruct a trailing car that is attempting to pass by

problem_area.question: A driver of a motor vehicle (excluding personal mobility devices) threatening another driver by repeatedly committing the acts below will be punished for reckless driving. Which of the following is NOT an example of reckless driving?
choice: Violation of traffic signals and instructions, driving over the centerline
Not securing sufficient safe distance, violating the prohibition on sudden braking
Violating the prohibition on obstructing attempts to pass, improperly overtaking a car
Violating the prohibition on passage, using a mobile phone while driving

problem_area.question: Which of the following is an act of reckless driving for which you can be penalized?
choice: Stopping or parking on the roadside of an expressway
Driving while under the influence of alcohol
Driving in an exclusive lane on a general road
Driving over the centerline

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT considered reckless driving for which you can be penalized?
choice: Violation of a traffic signal
Violation of speed limit
Violation of prohibition on driving motor vehicles in poor condition
Violation of prohibition of changing course

problem_area.question: A driver was arrested for causing special injury under the Criminal Act using vehicles, etc. (retaliatory driving). What is the administrative action for this person's driver's license?
choice: Cancellation of driver's license
Suspension of driver's license for 100 days
Suspension of driver's license for 60 days
No actions to be taken

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, what do you call the offense when the drivers of two or more cars (excluding personal mobility devices) drive side by side or in tandem on a road without valid reason, causing danger to other people?
choice: Dangerous collective conduct
Intersection tailgating
Cutting in
Disorderly conduct

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT subject to penalty for reckless driving?
choice: A driver who repeatedly brakes suddenly, causing danger in traffic
A driver who continually fails to maintain a safe distance from other drivers
A driver who repeatedly overtakes other vehicles in an illegal manner on the expressway
A driver who turns off his/her headlights and parks on the shoulder of the freeway at night, posing a danger to others

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a desirable driving habit?
choice: Turn off the engine while refueling.
Unless there is an urgent situation, do not apply the brake suddenly, instigating other vehicles to do the same.
To predict potential hazards and practice defensive driving, observe the speed limit and keep a safe distance.
To ensure driving stability, tire pressure should be 10% higher than the recommended standard regardless of the season.

problem_area.question: A driver was arrested for committing special violence under the Criminal Act using vehicles, etc. (retaliatory driving). What is the administrative action for this person's driver's license?
choice: Cancellation of driver's license
Suspension of driver's license for 100 days
Suspension of driver's license for 60 days
No administrative actions

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about a pedestrian?
choice: A person using a handcart (not motorized) that is 1 m or less in width is not considered a pedestrian.
A person using a rollator that is 1 m or less in width is considered a pedestrian.
A person riding a bicycle is not considered a pedestrian.
A person using a walking frame that is 1 m or less in width is considered a pedestrian.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a desirable attitude for a driver using a drive-thru store?
choice: Follow the safety instructions of the attendant at the drive-thru store.
Follow the instructions on the signage installed at the drive-thru store.
Stop by encroaching upon a crosswalk while waiting in line for the drive-thru store.
Exercise caution while navigating the entrance and exit areas of the drive-thru store, paying attention to safety facilities.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about a driver's duty to protect pedestrians?
choice: A traffic penalty will be imposed on the driver who fails to slow down or stop on a pedestrian-first road and obstructs the pedestrians.
The duty to protect pedestrians also applies to drivers driving on a place other than a road.
A driver who sees pedestrians crossing a crosswalk must come to a full stop before he/she reaches the crosswalk.
The driver must slow down when driving across a crosswalk in a school zone with no traffic lights.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about a driver's duty to protect pedestrians?
choice: A driver who sees pedestrians crossing a crosswalk must come to a full stop before he/she reaches the crosswalk.
The driver must slow down while keeping a safe distance when passing by pedestrians on a narrow road with no markings.
If a crosswalk in a school zone has no traffic lights, the driver has no duty to stop if there are no pedestrians crossing.
The driver should not obstruct pedestrians crossing an intersection with no traffic lights.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about pedestrian-first roads?
choice: On a pedestrian-first road, pedestrians can only walk along the right edge of the road.
Drivers are obligated to exercise various forms of pedestrian protection such as slowing down and coming to a complete stop.
A driver who fails to fulfill his/her duty to protect pedestrians is subject to a traffic penalty of KRW 40,000 and a penalty of 10 points based on the standards for passenger vehicles.
If deemed necessary for pedestrian protection, the chief of a police station can limit the vehicle speed to 20 km/h or less.

problem_area.question: While driving at 50 km/h on a city road, you find a pedestrian jaywalking. Which of the following is the most appropriate measure to take?
choice: Brake suddenly because the pedestrian is crossing the road.
Go past the pedestrian by predicting his/her movement.
Slow down your car, turn on the emergency flashers to notify the car behind you, and stop safely.
Slightly increase the speed to pass while warning the pedestrian by honking.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about a driver's duty to protect pedestrians?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, a child below the age of 13 should wear protective gear for safety when riding ( ). Which of the following does NOT fit in ( )?

In-line skates

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the duty to protect pedestrians?

It is okay to impede the passage of a pedestrian crossing a road with traffic signals.

problem_area.question: Which of the following persons or processions can pass through the center of a road?

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the safest way of driving a motor vehicle when passing through a crosswalk without traffic lights?

When there are no pedestrians crossing, proceed quickly.

problem_area.question: If a vehicle is stopped on the railroad crossing due to vehicle failure while passing through, which of the following is NOT proper behavior by the driver?

Use emergency signal devices.

problem_area.question: A vehicle is about to go across a sidewalk to enter a building. Which of the following is the correct sequence?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is an INCORRECT description of safe pedestrian crossing?

You must cross the road by using a crosswalk or such facilities even if you have a disability.

problem_area.question: Where does "disappearance"—a phenomenon wherein pedestrians or objects on roads vanish temporarily from the driver’s sight—occur most frequently?

Right side of the driver's lane
Around the centerline of the road

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding safe pedestrian crossing?

Disabled pedestrians must cross the road using road-crossing facilities where such facilities are present.
Where a sign prohibiting road crossing is present, pedestrians must cross the road quickly.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct part of a footpath that pedestrians should walk on?

The left side

problem_area.question: A vehicle is about to drive across a crosswalk in a school zone with no traffic lights. Which of the following are the correct actions of the driver? (Select TWO)

When there are pedestrians crossing, the driver must stop the vehicle and wait until all pedestrians have crossed.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO good pedestrian behaviors?

A pedestrian taking part in a parade for a special social event can walk in the middle of a road.
A disabled person who is unable to use road-crossing facilities can cross a road anywhere.
A pedestrian must take the longest route to cross the road if there are no road-crossing facilities.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the traffic penalty imposed on the driver of a passenger car for illegal driving on a sidewalk?

According to the Road Traffic Act, which is a case of violations by passenger vehicle drivers related to pedestrian safety and which have a different traffic penalty amount compared to other cases? (Excluding specific zones, e.g., school zones)
choice: Interrupting pedestrians crossing the road when the walk signal is lit
Entering a pedestrian-only road
Throwing an object onto the road from a moving vehicle
Failing to protect children, visually impaired, etc.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the right attitude that a driver should adopt toward pedestrians?

There is no need to pay attention to pedestrians on the side of the vehicle.
It is the pedestrians' responsibility to dodge vehicles.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, the part of the road marked with road safety signs that allow pedestrians to cross the road is called ( ).
choice: Sidewalk
Roadside area
Crosswalk

Drive slowly to protect pedestrians who are about to cross a crosswalk.

Pedestrians must not cross the road around the front or rear of a vehicle.

problem_area.question: What is the administrative fine imposed on the driver of a 4.5-ton truck parked illegally in a silver zone between 10 AM and 11 AM?

Pedestrians are allowed to use any part of the pedestrian-first road.
Pedestrians must walk on the sidewalk on roads where the sidewalk and roadways are separated.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT permitted to travel on a roadway?
choice: Cleaning or maintenance personnel working on roads
A person driving a large animal such as horse or cattle
A person who is pushing a stroller

problem_area.question: What are the grounds for the penalty faced by a driver who stops over the stop line when the traffic light has turned red?

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the right method of driving when you spot a visually impaired person crossing the road with a guide dog?

Stop momentarily.
Stop and help the person go back the way he/she came.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, the driver of any type of vehicle must ( ) before reaching a crosswalk in a school zone with no traffic lights, regardless of pedestrians crossing. Which of the following best fills the parentheses?

stop

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the protection of pedestrians crossing roads?

You have no responsibility to protect pedestrians crossing the road illegally.

problem_area.question: Who is NOT allowed to drive on the road according to the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A person using a wheelchair to aid his/her movement
A person marching on the streets for an important social event

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the best method of crossing the road for a pedestrian when the green light at the crosswalk is flashing?

Stand still in the middle of the crosswalk.
Cross the road without waiting for the next signal.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is correct regarding pedestrians using the sidewalk?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which TWO correctly describe the rules for a pedestrian-only road?

problem_area.question: When a cargo falls from a vehicle and injures a pedestrian, necessitating a two-week period of hospitalization, which two penalties among the following will be imposed on the driver?

No charges are filed if the driver comes to an agreement with the victim.
No charges are filed if the driver has a full-coverage insurance policy.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is the most appropriate method of crossing a road without a crosswalk for a pedestrian?

Cross the road quickly when there are no cars passing by.
Cross anywhere as there is no crosswalk.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT a correct explanation of how to traverse a crosswalk?
choice: A person may cross the crosswalk while pushing a personal mobility device.
A person may cross the crosswalk while on a motorized chair.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following methods of passage used by motor vehicles or horses is NOT correct?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is the most appropriate method of using a road with no clear distinction of a sidewalk but has a centerline for a pedestrian?

Walk along the right side of the roadway.
Walk along the roadside area.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is NOT the correct method of crossing when pedestrian signals are flashing?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is a situation wherein the driver of a motor vehicle must drive slowly?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, raised crosswalks are installed on roads where it is necessary to limit the speed to ( ) km/h or less. Which of the following is correct?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, the width of a lane installed shall be ( ) m or wider. problem_area.question: Below are the classifications of traffic by type of lane according to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree. Which of the following is INCORRECT?

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is INCORRECT regarding the method and speed to be followed by motor vehicles and horses?

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the maximum allowed speed for a truck with a 3-ton loading weight driving on an expressway with four lanes in one direction where the maximum allowed speed is 100 km/h?
