problem_area.question: In which of the following situations are motor vehicles and horses NOT allowed to pass along the left side of a road?

When the road is designated for one-way traffic

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most inappropriate method of passing an intersection and a Dilemma Zone?

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding the driving distance between vehicles?

Stopping distance is the distance necessary to avoid a rear-end collision with the car in front of you if it makes an abrupt stop.

problem_area.question: In which of the following areas is overtaking a vehicle possible?

Centerline (yellow broken line)
Inside the tunnel (white solid line)

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is the most appropriate method of slowing down at an intersection?

problem_area.question: Below are various explanations of the punishment criteria for driving a vehicle (except for personal mobility devices) in violation of the maximum speed on the road. Which of the following is correct?

Anyone who drives twice at a speed of over 100 km/h above the speed limit is subject to a criminal fine of up to KRW 3 million.
Anyone who drives at a speed of over 80 km/h above the speed limit is subject to a criminal fine of up to KRW 500,000 or detention.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of turning right at an intersection with no traffic lights?

Wait until the vehicles entering the intersection ahead of you pass by, and then turn right.
When a vehicle coming from the opposite direction is turning left, turn right at the same time with caution.

problem_area.question: A driver intends to turn right at an intersection with traffic signals but no auxiliary signals for vehicles. According to the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is not correct?

If the traffic signal has turned green, a right turn may be made without making a temporary stop in front of the stop line.
When the vehicle signal is a left-turn signal, you must come to a complete stop just before the stop line regardless of the pedestrian signal at the crosswalk.

problem_area.question: At an intersection, which of the following is the most correct method of turning left/right?

When turning left, you must pass through the outside of the intersection center.
When turning right, reduce speed in advance while driving along the right edge of the road.

problem_area.question: Which of the following correctly describes the stopping distance?

The distance that allows a driver to avoid colliding with a vehicle ahead when it makes a sudden stop
The distance traveled by a vehicle from the point at which the driver detects danger and steps on the brake pedal to the point at which the vehicle makes a complete stop
The distance from the point where a driver senses danger and presses the brake pedal to the point where the brake function actually starts to work

problem_area.question: What is the correct way of passing through an intersection?

When the yellow traffic light is flashing, drive through as quickly as you can.

problem_area.question: The following describes a Hi-pass lane. Which is correct?

Drivers must reduce speed in advance since the speed limit of a multi-lane Hi-pass toll road is 30 km/h.
Drivers must observe the speed limit of a multi-lane Hi-pass toll road. If the Hi-pass device malfunctions upon entry, drivers can pay the toll upon exit.

problem_area.question: What is the maximum speed for a passenger car traveling on an expressway under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: 60 km/h
70 km/h
80 km/h
90 km/h

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following speed limits can be placed on a general road in residential, commercial, and industrial areas?

Within 30 km/h
Within 40 km/h

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the policy that limits the maximum speed in the city center to 50 km/h while lowered to 30 km/h on back roads (including residential areas) in order to reduce traffic accidents?

Safety Speed 5030
Campaign for Unity in Order to Reduce Traffic Accidents
Intelligent Transportation System (ITS)

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the two correct actions a driver may take when making an unprotected left turn at an intersection?

Turn left at any time if there is no obstruction from oncoming vehicles, regardless of the signals.

problem_area.question: You are driving in a city that operates an exclusive median bus lane system. Which of the following are safe ways of driving? (Select TWO)
choice: You should continuously honk to prevent other vehicles from cutting in front of you.
You should drive with caution as pedestrians on the right may try to jaywalk.
You should slow down as pedestrians may cross the road from the bus station on the left.
You should drive and pass through a median bus lane as quickly as possible before the traffic light turns red.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following are TWO safe methods of changing lanes?
choice: Change lanes as quickly as possible.
You can change lanes only in those sections marked with solid white lines.
Change lanes without obstructing traffic.
You can change lanes only on a road marked with a white broken line.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO correct methods of turning right safely at an intersection?
choice: Use the turn indicator at least 30 m before where you want to turn right.
Move into the right-hand lane carefully if there is a solid white line on the road.
Turn right after checking your left side to make sure that there are no other vehicles headed in the same direction as you.
Turn right quickly while paying attention to the traffic in the area.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO correct methods for a passenger car driver to overtake another vehicle?
choice: Make sure that there is enough space on the left before overtaking.
Overtaking must be done within the speed limit of the road being traveled.
If it is safe to do so, overtaking another vehicle on its right hand side is acceptable.
After passing a vehicle on its left, move back into the lane when the vehicle that you just passed is no longer visible in the rear-view mirror.

problem_area.question: What are TWO safe driving methods when driving on the road?
choice: Park the vehicle in a safety zone if possible.
When the traffic lights turn yellow, stop inside the intersection to observe the traffic signal.
Maintain sufficient distance to prevent a collision with the vehicle ahead if it brakes suddenly.
When overtaking another vehicle, pass along its left side.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is not a motor vehicle that is required to install or place a fire extinguisher?
choice: A passenger car with 5 or more seats
A bus
A truck
A two-wheeled vehicle

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following measures must be taken when an emergency vehicle driving with an emergency siren on approaches?
choice: When an emergency vehicle approaches an intersection, drivers of other vehicles must stop at the left edge of the intersection.
On the road other than the intersection, other vehicles should yield so that emergency vehicles can pass first.
Drive faster than the emergency vehicle.
Stop temporarily and wait until the emergency vehicle passes.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the safest measure to take when a fire truck approaches with flashing red lights and siren while you are trying to turn right at an intersection?
choice: Temporarily stop, avoiding the intersection.
Stop right where you are.
Slow down, and turn right.
Pass through the intersection quickly and continue driving.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following privileges does NOT apply to emergency vehicles?
choice: Speed limit of motor vehicles
Restriction of overtaking
Restriction of cutting in
Ensuring pedestrian safety

problem_area.question: Emergency vehicles should be properly outfitted, and they should convey that they are undertaking an emergency operation by turning on their sirens or flashing lights. Which of the following vehicles is exempted from this obligation?
choice: Vehicles cracking down on illegal parking
Fire trucks
Ambulances
Police cars cracking down on violations of speed limit

problem_area.question: In order for fire trucks and ambulances to be granted a special privilege to overtake other vehicles or drive at a speed exceeding the limit in a no-passing zone, which of the following is the measure that the drivers of those vehicles should take?
choice: Drive while honking.
Equip the cars in a manner required for safe driving according to the Motor Vehicle Management Act and turn on the sirens or flashing lights.
Drive with the headlights on.
Those vehicles can be granted such privileges without taking additional measures.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is necessary for a general vehicle to be recognized as an emergency vehicle when transporting a patient in critical condition?
choice: The car should be authorized by the chief of the district police station.
The driver should turn on the headlights or emergency lights.
The car does not need to get special permission because it is transporting a patient in critical condition.
The car must be escorted by another vehicle.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which TWO are considered emergency vehicles?
choice: A fire truck going to a repair shop for repairs
A vehicle transporting patients who are injured or in a critical condition, or transporting blood for transfusion
An ambulance transporting a patient discharged from a medical institution
A vehicle authorized by the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency to transport urgent postal mail, etc.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following are TWO things that an ambulance driver is allowed to do while operating an emergency vehicle in an emergency situation?
choice: Continue to operate the vehicle after a traffic accident without administering first aid to the injured.
Continue to operate the vehicle even if it obstructs pedestrians from crossing.
Drive along the center or along the left side of the road.
Cut in front of other vehicles on a congested road.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, what is the reason a fire truck driver who is operating a vehicle for its original purpose cannot be granted exceptions as an emergency vehicle?
choice: Not wearing a seat belt
Drunk driving
Crossing of the centerline
Violation of traffic light

problem_area.question: The regular traffic safety education provided for drivers of emergency vehicles shall be taken every ( ) years. Which of the following best fits in ( )?
Which of the following is NOT true about an exception for emergency vehicles under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: They may overtake other vehicles at a location where passing is prohibited.
They may cut in front of other vehicles even where cutting is prohibited.
They may pass the crossroad even though there is a pedestrian crossing it without regard for their safety.
They may drive faster than the speed limit prescribed for the road.

problem_area.question: Stopping and parking are prohibited within ( ) meters of fire-extinguishing water supply system, emergency firefighting equipment, and firefighting systems under the Road Traffic Act. Which of the following best fills the parentheses?

Which of the following can be designated as an emergency vehicle by the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency at the request of the organization or the person intending to use the vehicle?
choice: A fire truck
A gasworks vehicle used for emergency gas leak repair
An ambulance
A blood bank vehicle

problem_area.question: Which of the following CANNOT be designated as an emergency vehicle by the commissioner of the City/Do police agency at the request of the organization or the person intending to use the vehicle?
choice: A police car used for traffic control
A vehicle used for delivery of urgent mail
A vehicle used for emergency work such as repair of phone lines
A civil defense vehicle used for emergency restorations

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT a situation wherein an emergency vehicle can flash its warning lights for non-emergency matters?
choice: When a fire truck is on patrol for fire prevention, rescue, and emergency activities
When a fire truck is being driven to the workshop for urgent repair
When a vehicle commissioned for civil defense activities participates in a drill relevant to its usual emergency responsibilities
When a police vehicle is on patrol for crime prevention and control

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following actions by an emergency vehicle on an emergency rescue mission is considered an offense?
choice: Driving at 100 km/h on a general road with two lanes in one direction
Overtaking a vehicle inside a tunnel where lanes are marked with a solid white line
Cutting in front of a vehicle at an intersection to turn right
Failing to report or take the necessary actions in the event of a traffic accident resulting in personal injury because the vehicle is on an emergency rescue mission

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT an instance wherein an emergency vehicle can use a warning light for non-emergency purposes?
choice: A fire truck on a round of patrol for fire prevention
A road maintenance vehicle on a round of patrol to prevent traffic accidents
An ambulance used in emergency response training
A police car on a round of patrol for crime prevention

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO correct methods of yielding to an emergency vehicle on the road?
choice: All vehicles must pull over to the left side of the road.
If the emergency vehicle is climbing up a narrow slope and you are facing it, the emergency vehicle must pull over to the right to make way for you.
Near an intersection, pull over at some distance from the intersection.
When an emergency vehicle approaches from a place other than an intersection or its vicinity, the roadway shall be yielded so that the emergency vehicle can take priority.

problem_area.question: Which TWO are considered emergency vehicles?
choice: A vehicle being guided by a police car for emergency purposes
An investigation vehicle used for non-investigatory purposes
A tow truck returning after completing a rescue activity
A vehicle transporting patients who are injured or in critical condition, or transporting blood for transfusion

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about the duty of a school bus driver/operator?
choice: A school bus driver must turn on the hazard lights only when children and infants are getting on or off the bus.
A school bus driver must make sure that all children and infants on board are wearing seat belts before moving the bus.
In case parents or guardians are on board together with the children, a school bus operator may post a sign indicating the accompaniment of parents/guardians.
In case parents or guardians are on board, there is no need to write safety operation records.

problem_area.question: In which location should the driver come to a full stop, regardless of the existence of pedestrians?
choice: A road with no centerline among the roads where the roadway and sidewalk are not distinguishable
Front of a crosswalk in a school zone with no traffic light
Pedestrian-first road
A place other than a road

problem_area.question: A school bus for children is driving in the first lane of a road with two lanes in one direction with markings to show that it has children or infants on board. Which of the following is the safest course of action?
choice: Do not overtake the bus even if the second lane is empty.
Overtake the bus using the second lane.
Honk to send a signal to make way.
Check the situation of the traffic in the opposite direction, and overtake the bus by crossing the centerline.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is correct regarding school zones?
choice: Areas in front of kindergartens or middle schools are designated as school zones.
Mayors and other officials can limit or prohibit vehicles from entering a school zone.
For the purposes of a school zone, children are defined as those under 12 years old.
A school zone can limit vehicles to a speed of 30 km/h.

problem_area.question: Under the Act on Special Cases Concerning the Settlement of Traffic Accidents, what is the appropriate punishment for a driver’s negligence resulting in injury to a child while driving at 40 km/h within a school zone?
choice: Only if demanded by the victim will the offender be subjected to criminal punishment.
Regardless of the victim's request, criminal punishment will be imposed.
When insured by comprehensive insurance, the driver will not face criminal punishment.
An agreement with the victim can prevent criminal punishment.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the allowed seating capacity for a vehicle to be registered as a school bus for children? (1 child is considered 1 passenger)
choice: 11-seater or larger
16-seater or larger
17-seater or larger
9-seater or larger

problem_area.question: What is the correct number of demerit points for the driver of a passenger car who has violated the speed limit by exceeding the maximum speed by 25 km/h at around 08:30 am in a school zone?
choice: 10 points
15 points
30 points
60 points

problem_area.question: How many demerit points are imposed on the driver of a passenger car who overtakes a school bus for children that is driving on a road with an indication that it has children or infants on board?
choice: 10 points
15 points
30 points
40 points

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, how many hours of safety education are required for persons subject to education regarding school buses for children?
choice: 1 hour or more
3 hours or more
5 hours or more
6 hours or more

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, who are considered children and infants?
choice: Those 13 years old or below are considered children.
Those who are below 6 years old are considered infants.
Those who are below 15 years old are considered children.
Those who are below 7 years old are considered infants.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct amount of traffic penalty that would be imposed on the driver of a passenger car who commits a traffic signal violation in a school zone at around 13:00?
Which of the following is the safest method of driving on a road where a child is playing alone without a guardian?
choice: The child should not be there in the first place. Just drive on.
Honk to scare the child away and drive past.
Stop temporarily.
Keep an eye on the child and speed away.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is a correct action taken by a driver when a child is crossing in a crosswalk?
choice: Upon noticing a pedestrian crossing the crosswalk, check for safety and pass slowly.
In the case of a crosswalk on a general road with no traffic light, the driver can pass the stop line as long as he/she does not enter the crosswalk area.
In the case of a crosswalk on a general road with no traffic light, the driver can drive slowly behind crossing children.
Pedestrian crossing signs are placed on both ends of a crosswalk, and all drivers must come to a full stop before reaching the crosswalk.

problem_area.question: If a school bus for children makes a stop at a road with one lane in one direction and switches on the flashers to indicate that infants are getting on and off the bus, which of the following is the correct behavior for a driver approaching from the opposite direction?
choice: Stop temporarily, check that it is safe, and drive on slowly.
Drive past at a reduced speed to ensure safety.
Continue to drive on.
Drive on while honking.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most appropriate behavior when a driver hits a child?
choice: A driver of a two-wheeled vehicle asked if the child was injured or not, but the child did not say anything. So the driver decided that the child was not injured and kept driving.
The driver of a passenger car stopped the vehicle immediately, visually checked the child, and concluded that the child was not injured and so kept driving.
The driver of a truck saw the child who was knocked over but got up immediately. Upon concluding that the child was not injured, the driver kept driving.
A cyclist saw the child who was knocked over but quickly got up and ran. The cyclist reported the case to the police station and waited at the scene.

problem_area.question: A vehicle just hit a child who suddenly emerged from an alleyway. The child appeared unharmed and claimed that he was fine. Which of the following is the correct action taken by the driver?
choice: The driver can leave since this is a crime that is not prosecuted against objection.
The driver can leave since the child was not hurt, so this is not considered an accident.
The driver must call the parents of the child and take other necessary measures before leaving the scene.
Since the fault lies with the child, the driver can just take the child's contact information and leave.

problem_area.question: Who is responsible for the designation and management of school zones under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Police chief
Mayor, etc.
Commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency
Superintendent of Education

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO correct statements about school zones?
choice: A school zone refers to a section of a road located within 100m of the main gate of an elementary school.
A traffic penalty can be imposed for cars that are stopped or parked illegally in a school zone between 8 AM and 8 PM.
The length of the green signal of traffic lights installed in a school zone is based on the fastest walking speed of an average child.
The demerit points applied to drivers who fail to consider pedestrian safety are doubled in school zones between 8 AM and 8 PM.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are true about the special protection for school buses? (Select TWO)
choice: The method of overtaking a school bus for children is the same as the method prescribed for overtaking other kinds of vehicles.
When children are getting on or off the bus, a car coming from the opposite direction on a road without a centerline should check that it is safe and drive slowly.
When children are getting on or off the bus, a car coming from the opposite direction on a road with one lane in one direction should stop momentarily, check that it is safe, and drive slowly.
When children are getting on or off the bus, vehicles in the same lane and adjacent lanes should stop momentarily, check that it is safe, and drive slowly.

problem_area.question: Below are descriptions of a thoroughfare of bicycles. According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is INCORRECT?
choice: The rider of a bicycle must pass along a bicycle track at a location with a separate bicycle track.
The rider of a bicycle must pass along close to the right side of the road at a location where no bicycle track is constructed.
The rider of a bicycle may pass along the roadside area (excluding a section where thoroughfare for bicycles is prohibited with safety markings).
A rider of a bicycle who intends to cross the road at a crosswalk may do so while riding the bicycle.

problem_area.question: Which ministries are responsible for jointly establishing the procedure and criteria for designating priority zones under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A school zone is jointly designated by the Ministry of Interior and Safety, Ministry of Health and Welfare, and Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport.
A silver zone is jointly designated by the Ministry of Interior and Safety, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport, and Ministry of Environment.
A priority zone for persons with disabilities is jointly designated by the Ministry of Interior and Safety, Ministry of Health and Welfare, and Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport.
A priority zone for people with mobility impairments is jointly designated by the Ministry of Interior and Safety, Ministry of Environment, and Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of driving considering the protection of school buses for children?
choice: If you are facing and approaching a school bus for children on a road with one lane in one direction, you must stop before reaching the bus, check that it is safe, and drive on slowly.
If a school bus for children has switched on flashers indicating that children are getting off, you must take another lane and drive past the bus quickly.
If there is no centerline and you are facing and approaching a school bus for children, you must keep driving at the same speed.
You can overtake a school bus for children, even if it has signs indicating that children are on board.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO correct statements regarding the registration of school buses for children?
choice: To operate a school bus, one must report to the Korea Road Traffic Authority beforehand and receive a report certificate.
A school bus for children must be a vehicle with maximum passenger capacity of nine or more (1 child is considered 1 passenger).
If applying to renew the registration of a school bus for children because the old one needs to be replaced, one must submit the old registration together with the renewal form.
The registration certificate of a school bus for children must be attached in a place where it will be easily visible on the top left side of the windows.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of facilitating the safe boarding and disembarking of a child for a driver operating a school bus for children?
choice: Flashers must be used to ensure safety while the child is boarding the vehicle.
If traffic is congested, turn off the flashers momentarily and allow the child to board.
Drop off children around the school bus and leave immediately.
Fastening a seatbelt is not required in a school zone.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about school zones?
choice: The traffic penalty for a parking violation is the same as that for the silver zone.
A "slow" sign can be installed in school zones.
Parking and stopping are prohibited within a school zone.
One will face a criminal penalty for causing an accident that leaves a child injured, regardless of any settlements.

problem_area.question: A child crosses a street in a school zone with an infant. Which of the following is the correct driving behavior?
choice: Stop temporarily to protect children and infants.
Slow down as a child crosses the street while protecting the infant.
Drive fast as the child and infant are on the opposite side of the road.
Since children and infants walk slowly, keep a safe distance and drive while avoiding them.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is correct regarding school zones?
choice: If a car accident occurs while a child is jaywalking, the driver is exempted from all liability.
If a cyclist hits a child while driving, only civil liability exists as a bicycle is not a vehicle.
Upon seeing a child suddenly running onto the road, stop the vehicle instead of reducing the speed.
The chief of a police station may set the speed limit of a vehicle at 50km or less per hour.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are true about school zones and driving in these zones? (Select TWO)
choice: A school zone may limit the vehicle speed to 30 km/h if necessary to ensure children's safety. Drivers must observe the speed limit when driving in a school zone.
In a school zone, drivers of motor vehicles, motorcycles, and trams must observe the speed limit and come to a full stop when children are crossing.
Roads near alternative or international schools are not designated as school zones; therefore, drivers must slow down when driving in these areas as children may be jaywalking.
Speed limit and pedestrian crossing signs can be placed with priority in school zones. Drivers must slow down when a child is standing by the centerline.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a violation of the special protection duty for school buses (on a one-lane road)?
choice: Passing through without stopping when children or toddlers are getting off.
Slowing down and passing through before reaching the bus, when approaching from the opposite direction.
Blowing the horn and passing through while children or toddlers are getting on or off.
Not overtaking a school bus displaying the child transport sign while driving.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is true about a child and an infant and the responsibilities of a school bus driver?
choice: Those 13 years old or below are considered children, and a school bus driver must ensure safety when children are getting on and off the bus.
A school bus driver must make sure that all children, except infants, have put on their seat belts prior to departure.
A school bus driver must safely drop off a child at the destination demanded by the child.
Those who are below 6 years old are considered infants, and a school bus driver must turn on the hazard lights when an infant is getting on and off the bus.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is true about the operation of the yellow and red strobe lights installed on a school bus for the purpose of allowing children or infants to get on or off the bus?
choice: The red indicator light(s) must be flashing when stopping the bus.
The red indicator light(s) must be flashing when braking.
The yellow indicator light(s) must be flashing when attempting to stop on the road.
Both yellow and red indicator light(s) must be flashing when parking.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a law that is the basis for designating a school zone?
choice: Early Childhood Education Act
Elementary and Secondary Education Act
Act on the Establishment and Operation of Private Teaching Institutes and Extracurricular Lessons
Child Welfare Act

problem_area.question: Below are descriptions related to school zones. Which of the following is NOT true?
choice: Traffic accidents in these areas may be excluded from the special exemption provided under the Special Traffic Accident Act.
The speed limit for vehicles can be set at 30 km/h or less.
The traffic penalty and demerit points are three times higher than those on general roads.
Parking and stopping are prohibited.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following children is NOT walking in a safe manner?
choice: A child walking on the edge of a roadway that is clearly separated from the sidewalk
A child walking on the edge of a one-way road while facing the oncoming traffic
A child walking on the edge of a roadway that does not have a clear separation from the sidewalk while facing the oncoming traffic
A child walking on the right-hand side of a sidewalk

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT correct regarding the protection of children?
choice: If a child is crossing a road without a crosswalk, drivers must drive slowly.
If a child is standing in a safety zone, drivers must drive slowly while maintaining a safe distance.
If a child is walking in a narrow alley, drivers must drive slowly while maintaining a safe distance.
If a child is crossing a crosswalk, drivers shall make a temporary stop in front of the crosswalk.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following correctly describes the driver's duty regarding the special protection of school buses?
choice: If a school bus for children with a red flasher being operated passes next to your lane, it is necessary to stop temporarily.
You cannot overtake any school bus for children passing on the road.
If you violate this duty, you will incur 15 demerit points on your driver's license.
This duty does not apply when a school bus for children stops on a road with one lane in one direction with its red flasher activited.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following constitutes a situation wherein a child’s guardian is slapped with an administrative fine?
choice: A guardian who lets a child ride a bicycle on the roadway
A guardian who lets a child ride an electric scooter in a playground
A guardian who lets a child ride an electric scooter on the roadway
A guardian who lets a child ride a bicycle in a playground

problem_area.question: What are the criminal penalties under the Act on the Aggravated Punishment, etc. of Specific Crimes for causing injury to a child in a child protection zone while driving?
choice: Imprisonment with labor for at least 1 year but not more than 15 years or a criminal fine of at least KRW 5 million but not more than KRW 30 million
Imprisonment with labor (or for an indefinite term) for at least 5 years
Imprisonment with labor for not more than 2 years or a criminal fine of not more than KRW 5 million
Imprisonment with labor for not more than 5 years or a criminal fine of not more than KRW 20 million

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the duty of a person who operates a school bus for children?
choice: When children are getting on a school bus, an adult guardian must be designated.
After completing the trip, the operator must confirm that all children and infants have disembarked from the school bus.
It is necessary to prepare and keep a record of seat belt wearing and confirmation of the accompanying guardian.
A record of confirmation of wearing seat belts and accompanying guardians must be submitted to the supervisory authority of the facility operating the school bus every quarter.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the punishment imposed on the operator when an “accompanying guardian sign” is attached even though there is no adult guardian on a school bus for children?
choice: A criminal fine of up to KRW 200,000 or detention
A criminal fine of up to KRW 300,000 or detention
A criminal fine of up to KRW 400,000 or detention
A criminal fine of up to KRW 500,000 or detention

problem_area.question: Which of the following is true about the definition of the senior driver sign under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A senior driver sign indicates that the vehicle is driven by a licensed driver aged 65 or older.
The background of the sign should be blue and the text should be yellow.
The front side should be made of rubber magnet for removal, and the back side should be made of reflective paper.
Attach it to the front of the vehicle in a visible location that does not interfere with safe driving.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT an authority that a commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency or a police chief can exercise in a silver zone?
choice: Prohibiting or restricting the passage of vehicles and horses
Designating and operating a back road as a one-way street
Setting a speed limit of 30 km/h for horses and vehicles
Designating an on-road parking lot for the elderly on a road next to the gate of a house

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following may be prohibited from entering or driving in a silver zone?
choice: Personal mobility devices (PMD), bicycles
Truck-mounted drilling rigs and kick scooters for children
Motorcycles and rollators with width of 1 m or less
Road stabilizers and walking frames with width of 1 m or less

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is true about a violation that took place in a silver zone at 10 AM?
choice: If the driver of a dump truck violates the traffic signal, the traffic penalty is KRW 130,000.
If the driver of a passenger vehicle obstructs a senior pedestrian, the traffic penalty is KRW 70,000.
If a bicycle rider obstructs a senior pedestrian crossing a crosswalk, the traffic penalty is KRW 50,000.
If the driver of a cultivator fails to fulfill his/her duty of guaranteeing pedestrian safety, the traffic penalty is KRW 30,000.

problem_area.question: Which of the following ministries is NOT authorized to enact enforcement ordinances regarding the procedures and criteria for the designation and abolition of senior or disabled protection zones?
choice: Ministry of Culture, Sports, and Tourism
Ministry of Interior and Safety
Ministry of Health and Welfare
Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, and Transport

problem_area.question: Which of the following officials CANNOT declare a silver zone?
choice: Mayor of a special metropolitan city
Mayor of a metropolitan city
Governor of a special self-governing province
A commissioner of a city/province (-do) police agency

problem_area.question: Which of the following is true about the general characteristics of senior citizens who are considered people with mobility impairments?
choice: Their reflexes are slow but they are quick in making decisions from experience.
They have poor eyesight but high hearing sensitivity.
They may not be quick in responding to unexpected situations, but their cognitive ability is heightened in such cases.
They lose mobility as their health conditions deteriorate.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is true about the measures that a mayor can take for a silver zone?
choice: The mayor can restrict or prohibit access by motor vehicles and horses and trams.
The mayor can prohibit access by buses, but cannot restrict access by trams.
The mayor can prohibit access by two-wheeled vehicles, but cannot restrict access by bicycles.
The mayor cannot prohibit access by heavy equipment, but can restrict such access.

problem_area.question: The driver of a passenger car slammed on the brakes after spotting an elderly person crossing a crosswalk without pedestrian signal lights but unfortunately hit him/her, and he/she was hospitalized for two weeks. Which TWO are correct among the following statements?
choice: The driver is not responsible at all as there were no pedestrian signal lights.
The driver is subject to civil liability.
Only the elderly person is subject to criminal prosecution for crossing illegally.
The driver is subject to criminal liability.

problem_area.question: Which TWO are applicable to the control that a police chief has over a silver zone?
choice: Prohibition or restriction of vehicle passage
Prohibition of stopping/parking
Installation of a road parking lot
Prohibition or restriction of pedestrian passage

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of driving when passing by an elderly person in a silver zone?
choice: Maintain a constant speed and drive by.
Maintain a safe distance and drive by slowly.
Honk and drive by quickly.
Flash the headlights and drive by.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a road facility that can be installed for the safety of the aged in a silver zone?
choice: Non-slip facilities, crash barriers
Speed bumps, non-slip facilities
Acceleration lane, silver zone sign
Crash barriers, road reflector system

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a safety precaution to be taken by a driver driving through a silver zone at night?
choice: Watch out for glare from another car’s headlights as you may not be able to see the pedestrians.
Drive at high speed because there are no elderly people around at night.
Drive through while looking out for any elderly crossing the road illegally.
Drive carefully because elderly people wearing black can be hard to spot.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about driving across a crosswalk with a traffic light in a silver zone and penalty for violation?
choice: When the traffic light turns green, the driver of a motor vehicle must look around first and speed up slowly.
If the driver of a passenger vehicle violates the traffic signal between 8 AM and 8 PM, the traffic penalty is KRW 120,000.
A bicycle rider must also wait for all senior pedestrians to finish crossing the street.
If a motorcycle rider obstructs pedestrians who are crossing at a crosswalk between 8 a.m. and 8 p.m., a traffic penalty of KRW 90,000 will be imposed.

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements is INCORRECT regarding a silver zone?
choice: A traffic penalty is imposed for speeding between 8 AM and 8 PM.
Green traffic lights for pedestrians are longer that those on general roadways.
No road parking lots can be installed.
Larger traffic lights are installed so that they can be seen more easily by the elderly.

problem_area.question: How many demerit points does the driver of a passenger car incur if caught driving at a speed that is 25 km/h faster than the limit in a silver zone at around 11 AM?
choice: 60 points
40 points
30 points
15 points

problem_area.question: Which of the following is an INCORRECT method of driving when approaching a crosswalk with traffic lights in a silver zone?
choice: If an elderly person crosses the road after the signals change, wait for the person to finish crossing before taking off.
Drivers must anticipate signal changes and drive accordingly to ensure safe driving.
Drive slowly to come to a safe stop when the signals change.
Take extra precautions as the elderly walk at a slower pace.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is INCORRECT regarding the measures applicable to a silver zone?
choice: The speed limit can be set to 30 km/h.
A "caution" sign can be installed as the duration of the pedestrian signal is the same as that of the normal pedestrian signal.
Extra traffic safety installations, e.g., speed bumps, can be added.

Signal devices must be prioritized for installation and maintenance at crosswalks on arterial roads located closest to the main entrance of the designated facility within the specific zone (e.g., school zone).

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT a behavior for which a traffic penalty is imposed when it occurs in a silver zone between 8 AM and 8 PM?
choice: Violation of traffic signal
Violation of parking rules
Obstruction of pedestrians in a crosswalk
Invasion of the centerline

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT true about a silver zone?
choice: When driving in a silver zone, look around with caution for any potential hazards.
A silver zone sign is a road sign that informs drivers of their duty to protect the seniors within the designated zone.
A silver zone sign is installed in the center of the road within said zone.
If the driver of a passenger vehicle obstructs a pedestrian crossing a crosswalk in a silver zone at 10 AM, the traffic penalty is KRW 120,000.

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements about senior protection zones under the Road Traffic Act is incorrect?
choice: The authority to designate, cancel, and manage such zones lies with the mayor, governor, or local administrator.
The local police chief may designate and operate side streets as one-way roads.
The local police chief may restrict traffic within senior protection zones.
The mayor or governor may limit the driving speed to 30 km/h or less.

problem_area.question: Who among the following pedestrians is most likely to comply with the Road Traffic Act?
choice: A pedestrian who crosses a road without a crosswalk using the shortest possible distance.
A pedestrian who crosses a road without using a crosswalk, as there are no cars passing by.
A pedestrian who crosses a road behind a stopped truck.
A pedestrian who walks on the left side of a sidewalk.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following driving behaviors by an elderly driver is subject to the highest demerit points?
choice: Stop the car at the crosswalk and obstruct the passage of pedestrians.
Notice a pedestrian late and apply the brake so suddenly that the pedestrian almost fell over.
Upon spotting a pedestrian who is jaywalking, honk and pass quickly in front of the pedestrian.
Overtake the pedestrian by crossing the solid yellow centerline.

problem_area.question: According to the Act on the Promotion of the Transportation Convenience of Mobility Disadvantaged Persons, which of the following persons is not regarded as a "mobility disadvantaged person"?
choice: A child
An elderly person
A teenager
A pregnant woman

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the general physical characteristics of a senior citizen?
choice: Slow movement
Vision deteriorates, but hearing improves
Slow reflexes
Physical strength is weakened

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most desirable driving behavior of an elderly driver?
choice: Always drive slowly for safety reasons when traveling long distances, regardless of the type of road.
Drive a vehicle without taking a rest until you reach your destination to save time.
Keep an eye on the road situation and drive by complying with the speed limit.
Drive mainly at night when there is less traffic.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the least related to safe driving by a senior citizen?
choice: Taking sufficient rest prior to driving
Periodic checks of health condition
Verification of the route to the destination prior to driving
Encouraging nighttime driving rather than daytime driving.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT the correct explanation regarding criminal punishment if a driver of a passenger car causes an injury that will take 3 weeks to heal to an elderly person in the silver zone for failing to look ahead while driving?
choice: If a driver has comprehensive insurance, he/she is not subject to criminal punishment.
Criminal punishment is applied when there is a road sign indicating a silver zone.
If a victim does not want punishment, a driver is not subject to criminal punishment.
If both parties reach an agreement, criminal punishment is not imposed.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is in the correct range of traffic penalties and demerit points (excluding surcharges) imposed on a car driver who drives above 60 km/h at around 15:00 in a silver zone?
choice: KRW 60,000, 60 points
KRW 90,000, 60 points
KRW 120,000, 120 points
KRW 150,000, 120 points

problem_area.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding parking spaces designated for persons with disabilities?
choice: A vehicle with a tag permitting parking in parking spaces reserved for persons with disabilities may park there even if a disabled person is not riding in the vehicle.

If a person issued with a tag permitting parking in parking spaces reserved for persons with disabilities uses it in an unlawful manner such as transfer or lending, the tag may be confiscated or reissuance may be restricted.
Any act impeding parking in parking spaces reserved for persons with disabilities, such as piling up items or blocking passage, is strictly prohibited.
When a vehicle without a parking tag is parked in a parking space reserved for persons with disabilities, an administrative fine of KRW 100,000 is imposed.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is not an agency that issues a parking tag for parking spaces reserved for persons with disabilities?
choice: Ministry of Patriots and Veterans Affairs
Special Self-Governing City Mayor, Special Self-Governing Province Governor
Head of Si/Gun/Gu
Minister of Health and Welfare

problem_area.question: Which of the following lights must be turned on when stopping on a road at night (except for two-wheeled vehicles)?
choice: Headlights and taillights
Indoor lights and sidelights
License plate lights and headlights
Taillights and sidelights

problem_area.question: Which of the following descriptions is most correct regarding the yellow broken line on the edge of a road?
choice: Both parking and stopping are allowed on the edge of a road marked with yellow broken lines.
Parking is prohibited, but stopping is allowed on the edge of a road marked with yellow broken lines.
Parking is allowed, but stopping is prohibited on the edge of a road marked with yellow broken lines.
Both parking and stopping are prohibited on the edge of a road marked with yellow broken lines.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about idling and parking restrictions for a personal mobility device?
choice: An area located within 10 m from the edge of an intersection or an area located within 5 m from the corner of a road
An area located within 10 m from a crosswalk or an area located within 10 m from the edge of a railway crossing
An area located within 10 m from any side of a safety zone or an area located within 10 m from bus stop columns
An area located within 5 m from a place where emergency firefighting equipment is installed or an area located within 5 m from a fire-extinguishing water supply system

problem_area.question: What is the administrative fine for parking a non-EV in the charging area of an eco-friendly vehicle?
Which of the following correctly describes a Dutch Reach (method of opening the door) when exiting a vehicle?
choice: When exiting the vehicle, grab the handle with the hand farthest from the window, check if there is oncoming traffic from the rear, and open the door.
When exiting the vehicle, grab the handle with the hand closest to the window, check if there is oncoming traffic from the front, and open the door.
The Dutch Reach prevents the driver from moving the vehicle with a door open.
The Dutch Reach is a traffic safety campaign started in the UK.

problem_area.question: How long can an EV or a plug-in hybrid vehicle park at a charging station after commencing charging?
choice: 1 hour
2 hours
3 hours
4 hours

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements about parking and stopping on a slope under the Road Traffic Act is incorrect?
choice: When parking, operate the parking brake and use wheel chocks or blocks to prevent the vehicle from slipping.
When stopping, if the driver remains seated and directly operates the brake, no additional anti-slip measures are required.
When parking, operate the parking brake and turn the steering wheel toward the road.
The parking brake alone is insufficient when stopping and leaving the driver’s seat temporarily; rubber or plastic must be used to prevent the vehicle from slipping.

problem_area.question: Which one of the following is the correct amount of administrative fine imposed on those who stack objects on or obstruct the entry to parking spaces reserved for persons with disabilities?
Which of the following are TWO statements correctly describe how a driver should behave?
choice: The passenger is responsible for his/her safety when exiting the vehicle.
Even when parking temporarily to buy something, the driver must turn off the engine.
Before exiting a vehicle, the driver must turn off the engine and check that it is safe.
When parking in an area other than a parking lot, the parking brake must be left released so that the vehicle can be moved if necessary.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO safest methods of parking on a steeply sloped road?
choice: Engage the parking brake and put the vehicle into reverse gear, regardless of the direction of the slope.
Engage the parking brake and turn the steering wheel toward the roadside (meaning toward the side closest to the vehicle).
Engage the parking brake and place a wheel stopper or other device that can prevent the vehicle from slipping in the downhill direction.
For manual transmission vehicles, engage the parking brake and leave the gear in neutral.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO correct statements about stopping/parking?
choice: When stopping on a road with a separate sidewalk, park at the far right edge of the roadway.
There is no need to follow the stopping/parking instructions shown on safety signs.
In the case of manual vehicles, you may leave the vehicle in 1st or reverse gear without using the parking brake when parking on level ground.
On sloped roads, engage the parking brake and place a wheel stopper.

problem_area.question: Which TWO constitute parking?
choice: A vehicle remaining stationary after breaking down
A temporary stop after recognizing a safety hazard
A stopped vehicle that is left unattended by the driver so that it cannot be driven immediately, even if it is for less than five minutes
Stopping briefly near a subway station to drop off a friend

problem_area.question: Which TWO constitute stopping?
choice: A taxi stopping at a taxi stand for the driver to go to the restroom
A vehicle that is left unattended by its driver who is away to pick up a load to transport
The vehicle stops while waiting for a traffic signal.
The driver stops the car to ask a passerby for directions.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following are TWO locations that allow a motor vehicle to make a stop or park?
choice: Within 50 m from the main entrance in a school zone
Within 10 m from a crosswalk
Within a distance of 5 m from where emergency fire-extinguishing equipment is installed
Within 10 m from a safety zone in all four directions

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following are TWO locations that allow a motor vehicle to park?
choice: 5 m from the corner of a road
At a distance of 7 m or more from where a fire-extinguishing water facility is installed
At a distance of 7 m or more from where emergency fire-extinguishing equipment is installed
5 m from a safety zone

problem_area.question: At an intersection with no traffic control, what is the safest method of turning left?
choice: Drive slowly and turn left with care even if there are other vehicles that have entered the intersection ahead of you.
Yield to cars entering from wider roads.
Yield to cars passing through straight across, but take the right of way over cars trying to turn right.
Go slowly along the centerline of the road beforehand and turn left using the outside center of the intersection.

problem_area.question: Which of the following INCORRECTLY describes a safe method of using a roundabout?
choice: Slow down before entering a roundabout.
Stop temporarily at the yield line and enter slowly.
A vehicle entering the roundabout has the right of way, so the vehicle making a turn in the roundabout must yield.
Turn counterclockwise.

problem_area.question: A vehicle that has entered an intersection without traffic lights ahead of you is turning left. What should you do?
choice: Proceed with caution if you are going to pass straight through.
If you are going to turn right, do so at low speed.
If you are going to go straight through or turn right, yield to vehicles turning left.
If you are driving on a narrow road, pass through the intersection at low speed.

problem_area.question: What is the most appropriate method of turning left at an intersection under the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Slow down along the centerline and turn left through the inner side of the intersection’s center.
Left turns are allowed at a red light at a signalized intersection if there is an “unprotected left turn” sign.
At a signalized intersection, a left-turning vehicle has priority over a vehicle traveling straight ahead.
On a one-way road with three lanes, turning left from the right lane is allowed if the traffic is light.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the proper method of passing through an intersection without traffic control?
choice: Drive slowly when it is impossible to check the sides.
A vehicle turning left has the right of way over a vehicle traveling straight.
A vehicle turning right has the right of way over a vehicle traveling straight.
You should drive slowly when the intersection is wider than the road you are on.

problem_area.question: In which of the following locations is the installation of a roundabout recommended for ensuring smooth traffic flow and safety on the road?
choice: Locations where traffic volumes are not very high, but there are many intersection accidents
Locations where there are one or more access roads at the intersection with three or more lanes in one direction
Locations where the traffic volume at the roundabout exceeds its capacity
Locations where the presence of a roundabout in a segment necessary for signal coordination would reduce the effectiveness of coordination

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes a roundabout?
choice: Roundabouts have more conflicting points than signalized intersections.
When entering, you must wait at the yield line until there is enough space within the roundabout.
Traffic lights are installed at a roundabout to guide vehicles and facilitate traffic flow through the intersection.
A vehicle that has already entered a roundabout must yield to an entering vehicle.

problem_area.question: What is the term used to refer to the road surface markings of white broken lines designed to guide left turns at intersections?
choice: Turn guidelines
Extension lines
Instruction lines
Regulation lines

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO correct methods of turning left safely at an intersection?
choice: Watch out for stopping vehicles from the opposite direction.
Watch out for right-turning vehicles from the opposite direction.
Watch out for right-turning vehicles from the same direction.
Watch out for left-turning vehicles in the lane to your side.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the TWO safest methods of turning left/right at an intersection?
choice: To turn right, take the rightmost lane of the road and slowly turn right.
To turn left on a congested road, disregard the turn guidelines on the left and drive quickly.
To turn left, drive slowly along the centerline and turn left along the inner part of the intersection.
To turn left at an intersection marked with turn guidelines, turn with the left tires passing through the inside of the turn guidelines.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a correct guideline with regard to passing through a roundabout?
choice: A car inside the roundabout has the right of way.
A car about to enter the roundabout has the right of way.
A car exiting the roundabout has the right of way.
There are no set rules on who has the right of way.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT prohibited at a roundabout?
choice: Stopping
Parking
Driving slowly and stopping temporarily
Overtaking

problem_area.question: Which of the following vehicles are granted the right of way at a roundabout?
choice: Vehicles moving on the section of the road within the roundabout
Vehicles trying to turn left before entering the roundabout
Vehicles trying to turn right before entering the roundabout
Vehicles trying to turn left or right before entering the roundabout

problem_area.question: Which of the following is an INCORRECT guideline on how to use roundabouts?
choice: Vehicles must approach the roundabout slowly.
A car inside the roundabout has the right of way.
Vehicles may continually enter the roundabout as there are no signals.
Turn clockwise in the roundabout.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the most appropriate ways of navigating a roundabout? (Select TWO)
choice: Before entering a roundabout, come to a complete stop and enter when there are no approaching vehicles from the left.
At a roundabout, the traffic circulates clockwise.
There is no need to use turn signals when entering or exiting a roundabout.
Even after entering a roundabout, you should continue to be cautious of other vehicles and proceed with care.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a place where you should stop temporarily while driving?
choice: Near a curve on a road
Descent on a steep slope
Near the top of a slope
At an intersection with no traffic control and heavy traffic

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is a place where you should stop temporarily?
choice: An intersection without a traffic control officer where traffic to the left and right cannot be checked
An intersection where the lights have turned green
A busy bridge/tunnel
In the vicinity of a curve or the highest point on a slope

problem_area.question: Which of the following is a place where you should stop temporarily pursuant to the Road Traffic Act?
choice: Inside a tunnel and on a bridge
At a busy intersection with no traffic lights
On a descending section of a steep hill
A curved section of a road

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding the variable speed limit section?
choice: It is an active system that changes the regulated speed in accordance with the situation.
It uses an electric sign that can display different speeds by changing the numbers.
When the maximum speed is set with a variable speed limit sign, it must be followed.
When the maximum speed determined by the variable speed limit sign and the maximum speed of the safety sign show different values, the maximum speed indicated by the safety sign must be followed.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, the driver of ( ) must ( ) in front of a railway crossing and then pass through after confirming that it is safe to cross. Which of the following best fills the parentheses?
choice: any vehicle - slow down
any motor vehicle or heavy equipment - slow down
any vehicle or tank - stop
any vehicle or tram - stop

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the safest method of driving at an expressway interchange?
choice: As interchanges tend to be congested, apply sudden brakes to discourage vehicles behind you from following closely and prevent potential accidents accordingly.
As drivers tend to become less aware of speed at an interchange, stop momentarily before continuing.
If the desired interchange has already been passed, use the next interchange.
Accelerate quickly to enter an interchange.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the most correct action to take when the driver of the vehicle ahead stretches his/her arm horizontally out of the left side of the vehicle?
choice: Slow down as the front vehicle is expected to turn right.
Switch to an upper lane as the front vehicle is expected to cross.
Overtake the front vehicle as it is expected to make a U-turn.
Slow down as the front vehicle is expected to change lanes.

problem_area.question: Which of the following hand signals is used to indicate your intent to turn right?
choice: Extend your left arm out to the left and bend your forearm vertically upward.
Extend your left arm out to the left, parallel to the road.
Extend your right arm out to the right and hold it parallel to the road, and wave the extended arm forwards and backwards.
Stick your left arm out of the vehicle and bend your forearm downward at an angle of 45°.

problem_area.question: As a vehicle is about to enter an intersection according to the traffic signal, a police officer gives a hand signal to stop. In this scenario, which of the following is the safest way to drive?
choice: Stop just before the stop line.
Decelerate suddenly to slow down.
Proceed according to the signal from the traffic light.
Enter the intersection slowly.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is true about overtaking on a road with a combination of dotted yellow and solid yellow centerlines?
choice: Overtaking is not allowed from either the yellow broken line or the yellow solid line.
Overtaking is allowed from the side of the yellow broken line.
Overtaking is allowed from the sides of both yellow broken line and yellow solid line if it is clear that it is safe.
Overtaking is allowed from the side of the yellow solid line.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of driving through a railway crossing?
choice: Drive through if there are no trains coming.
Come to a stop, check that it is safe, and drive through.
Drive through at a speed above the speed limit.
Drive at an increased speed if the crossing gate is about to close.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the correct method of turning left?
choice: Turn on the left turn signal at least 30m (100m on expressways) away from where you want to turn left.
Turn on the left turn signal at least 10m (100m on expressways) away from where you want to turn left.
Turn on the left turn signal at least 20m (80m on expressways) away from where you want to turn left.
Turn on the left turn signal when you reach the location where you want to turn left.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT a correct statement regarding the speed of motor vehicles, etc.?
choice: Separate legal speed limits are applied to various roads depending on whether it is a general road, an automobile road, or an expressway, and on the total number of lanes.
There are no minimum speed limits on regular roads.
You must drive at a reduced speed in poor weather conditions.
If the speed limit set by the variable speed limit sign and the speed limit set by a speed limit safety sign are different, the latter must be followed.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT a correct statement regarding the speed of motor vehicles, etc.?
choice: Speed limits are set because the likelihood of traffic accidents increases at higher vehicle speeds.
Minimum speed limits are set to maintain the efficiency of automobile roads and expressways.
Provisional speed limits can be set by the commissioner general of the Korean National Police Agency, or the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency for road safety and efficiency.

For the setting of speed limits on expressways, the commissioner of the City/Do (Province) police agency has jurisdiction; for all other roads, the commissioner general of the Korean National Police Agency has jurisdiction.

problem_area.question: Which TWO constitute a violation of a traffic signal?
choice: Stopping over the stop line at a red light
Entering an intersection when the lights have already turned yellow
When there is a yellow flashing light, you must drive carefully while being mindful of other traffic markings or safety signs
Stopping temporarily in front of the stop line when the lights are blinking red, before driving through carefully

problem_area.question: Which of the following are the correct methods of turning right at an intersection on a road with three lanes in one direction? (Select TWO)
choice: When turning right, you should turn on the turning signal at the intersection just before proceeding to indicate your intended direction of travel.
Even if the cross light at the right-side crosswalk is green, you can proceed if there are no pedestrians.
If the right-turn signal is red, you cannot proceed even if there are no pedestrians.
It is safer to turn right from the second lane on a road with three lanes in one direction.

problem_area.question: According to the Motor Vehicle Management Act, which of the following are true about the criteria for a bus and the precautions when turning left behind a bus? (Select TWO)
choice: A bus refers to a vehicle with seating capacity of 36 or more persons. When driving behind a bus, you must drive slowly, keeping a safe distance from the bus so that you can see the traffic lights.

A mini-bus refers to a vehicle with seating capacity of 16 ~ 35 persons. When you are driving behind a mini-bus and the mini-bus driver turns on a turn signal, slow down and catch up to the mini-bus so that other vehicles will not cut in front of you.

A van refers to a vehicle with seating capacity of 15 or fewer persons. Since vans are more prone to overturning due to their high center of gravity compared to passenger vehicles, you must keep a safe distance from them.
A light van refers to a vehicle with engine displacement below 1,200 cc. Although light vans have the same center of gravity as passenger vehicles, you must keep a safe distance from them.

problem_area.question: Which TWO correctly describe how to change lanes?
choice: Change lanes while maintaining speed if the car behind you in the lane you are about to enter is far back enough.
Slow down when changing lanes if the car behind you in the lane you are about to enter is far back enough.
Let the car behind you in the lane you are about to enter pass if it is approaching.
Change lanes quickly if the car behind you in the lane you are about to enter is approaching.

problem_area.question: Which of the following are TWO correct statements about the lines that separate lanes?
choice: Where a solid line and a broken line run parallel, you cannot change from the lane near the solid-line to the lane near the broken-line.
Where a solid line and a broken line run parallel, you can change from the lane near the solid line to the lane near the broken line.
Where a solid line and a broken line run parallel, you cannot change from the lane near the broken line to the lane near the solid line.
Where a solid line and a broken line run parallel, you can change from the lane near the broken line to the lane near the solid line.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, what does a flashing red light indicate?
choice: Vehicles and horses must drive slowly while looking out for traffic.
Vehicles and horses may proceed while looking out for traffic.
Vehicles and horses may reverse while looking out for safety signs.
Vehicles and horses must temporarily stop right before the stop line and then proceed while looking out for traffic.

problem_area.question: Which of the following instructions about an intersection with an unprotected left-turn sign is correct?
choice: You can turn left regardless of the signal as long as you look out for traffic.
You can turn left at a red light while looking out for traffic.
You can turn left at a green light while looking out for traffic.
You can turn left at a yellow light while looking out for traffic.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is a correct statement regarding the speed of motor vehicles?
choice: The minimum speed limit on expressways is 50 km/h.
Automobile roads have a maximum speed limit, but no minimum speed limit.
There are maximum and minimum speed limits on normal roads.
The maximum speed limit on expressways with more than two lanes in one direction is the same for all types of vehicles.

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements regarding overtaking is correct?
choice: You can overtake a vehicle that is in the process of overtaking another vehicle.
You must overtake on the right when in a tunnel.
Overtaking on a road with one lane in one direction is permitted only in an area with solid yellow lines.
Overtaking is prohibited in intersections.

problem_area.question: Which of the following statements regarding the centerline on a road is correct?
choice: Overtaking is allowed if the center is marked with a yellow solid line.
The centerline of a reversible lane is the leftmost yellow broken line in the direction of traffic indicated by traffic signals.
A bus stopped on a provincial road with one lane in one direction can be overtaken by crossing the yellow solid centerline.
A centerline can be installed on roads at least 4.75 m wide.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following vehicles may drive along the second lane ofan expressway with three lanes in one direction?
choice: Trucks
Special vehicles
Vehicles fitted with construction equipment
Small and mid-sized passenger vehicles

problem_area.question: Under the Road Traffic Act, on an expressway with two lanes in one direction, if the first lane must inevitably be used with speed limit set to less than ( ) km/h due to an increase in traffic volume, vehicles are allowed to drive in the first lane even if they are not overtaking. Which of the following fills in the blank correctly?
Which of the following is true about using an expressway shoulder?
choice: If drowsy while driving, you can pull over on the shoulder to take a rest.
You can park on the shoulder to appreciate a good sunrise.
You can drive on the shoulder when the freeway is congested.
The traffic penalty for driving on the shoulder without good cause is a fine of KRW 60,000.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, on an expressway with five lanes in one direction, up to which lane is considered the left lane based on the standard of traffic flow in each lane? (Assuming smooth traffic flow and no designated lanes or acceleration/deceleration lanes)
choice: Lanes 1 to 2
Lanes 2 to 3
Lanes 1 to 3
The second lane only

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is NOT true about a designated lane on an expressway? (Assuming smooth traffic flow and no exclusive bus lanes)
choice: Lane 1 of an expressway with three lanes in one direction can be used by passenger vehicles, light vans, vans, mini-buses, and buses to overtake other vehicles.
You can use the lane on the left of the designated lane to overtake another vehicle.
All vehicles can use the lane on the left of their respective designated lanes.
The penalty for not using the designated lane for drivers of passenger vehicles is 10 points.

problem_area.question: Under the Road Traffic Act, which of the following statements regarding the method of overtaking by passenger cars on a smoothly flowing three-lane one-way highway is INCORRECT? (No exclusive bus lanes)
choice: To overtake, a passenger car switches to Lane 1 and continues in Lane 1.
A large passenger vehicle in Lane 3 switches to Lane 2 in order to overtake.
A small van driving in the second lane should move into Lane 1 in order to overtake.
A 5-ton truck uses Lane 2 to overtake.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is an INCORRECT guideline for the use of the right traffic lanes on roadways?
choice: All vehicles may use the right lane of the designated lane.
A passenger car overtaking another vehicle shall use the lane to the immediate right of the lane designated by traffic standards.
On a general road with four lanes in one direction, a passenger car may drive in any of the lanes.
On an expressway with four lanes in one direction, a large truck is to use the lane on the right.

problem_area.question: Which among the following is permitted to use the exclusive bus lane on a general roadway?
choice: A 12-seater passenger vehicle carrying 6 passengers
A passenger vehicle for domestic tourists that is transporting 25 tourists
A 12-seater commuter passenger vehicle carrying employees on designated routes
A taxi temporarily using the lane so that passengers can board/leave the taxi

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, a passenger car with 9 seats can take the exclusive bus lane on the expressway if ( ) or more people are on the vehicle. Which of the following is correct?
What is the standard for driving on an expressway with three lanes in one direction? (Assuming smooth traffic flow and no exclusive bus lanes)
choice: Passenger cars are to use Lane 1 so they must use only Lane 1.
A small passenger vehicle whose designated lane is Lane 2 should use Lane 1 when overtaking.
Large vans must use the first lane.
A truck with maximum load weight of 1.5 tons or less must drive in Lane 1.

problem_area.question: A passenger vehicle is driving in Lane 2 on an expressway with three lanes in one direction according to the Road Traffic Act. What is the correct lane for overtaking? (Assuming smooth traffic flow and no exclusive bus lanes)
choice: The first lane
The second lane
The third lane
Any of the above

problem_area.question: Which of the following is NOT correct regarding overtaking according to the Road Traffic Act?
choice: To overtake another vehicle, you must pass on the left lane of the vehicle in front.
If the centerline is a yellow broken line, it is possible to overtake when there is no vehicle in the opposite direction.
In case of a reversible lane, overtaking is not allowed on the leftmost yellow broken line in the direction indicated by a traffic light.
On an expressway with four lanes in one direction, vehicles driving on the right lane can move up to the first lane.

problem_area.question: Below are explanations of the standard for driving according to the lane under the Road Traffic Act. Which of the following is NOT true? (Expressways with smooth traffic flow and no exclusive bus lanes)
choice: When proceeding at a low speed, the driver may use the lane to the right of the current lane in which he/she has been driving.
The first lane of an expressway with two lanes in one direction may be used by all motor vehicles to overtake.
On a one-way traffic road, the rightmost lane is considered the first lane.
The right lane of an expressway with three lanes in one direction is to be used by a truck to drive.

problem_area.question: According to the Road Traffic Act, which of the following is true about the standard for driving on an expressway with three lanes in one direction? (Assuming smooth traffic flow and no exclusive bus lanes)
choice: The first lane is the lane for overtaking a passenger car driving on the second lane.
The first lane is the driving lane for a van.
The roadside is the driving lane for emergency vehicles and tow vehicles.
When operating an exclusive bus lane, the first lane is the driving lane for trucks.

problem_area.question: According to the definition provided by the Road Traffic Act and its Enforcement Decree, which of the following is NOT a type of exclusive lane?
choice: An exclusive lane for buses
An exclusive lane for high-occupancy vehicles
An exclusive lane for motor vehicles
An exclusive lane for bicycles

problem_area.question: Which of the following describes aquaplaning most appropriately?
choice: You must change tires with wider widths to reduce the risks associated with aquaplaning.
Aquaplaning is more common in snow than rain. You must reduce speed.
Tires with worn-down treads will experience less aquaplaning due to reduced traction.
Underinflation can cause the tires to aquaplane more, especially at higher speeds.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the safe method of driving when a vehicle loses control on a frozen road?
choice: Steer into the skid.
In the case of a car with manual transmission, shift to high gear.
Operate the steering wheel in the opposite direction of the skid.
Stop the car using the parking brake.

problem_area.question: Which of the following is the safest method of driving a vehicle on a foggy road?
choice: On a curved road or at an intersection, honk to warn other vehicles to make way and proceed quickly.
In dense fog, keep the high-beam headlights on to secure visibility.
On a foggy road, switch on the fog light only.
When visibility is secured to some extent, drive slowly while monitoring the guard rail, centerline, and traffic lane to stay aware of the vehicle's position.
